item.title: Guided Meditation: Stabilizing; Compassion
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation that begins with settling the body in a comfortable, alert posture and gently questioning the habit of vigilance. The practice moves into sensing breath throughout the body, especially neglected or tense areas, before opening into compassion for one’s own pain. He emphasizes a patient, steady love that does not try to erase suffering, but meets it with care and presence.
Chapters: 00:09 Settling In 01:01 Letting Go of Vigilance 02:53 Kind Intention 04:22 Breath in the Body 06:00 Unifying Numb Areas 07:53 Soothing Intensity 12:19 Turning Toward Compassion 18:42 Words of Care 24:23 Patient Love
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21564

item.title: Guided Meditation: In the Presence of Love (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by settling the body with fuller breaths and a felt sense of love, then repeatedly returns to gravity, breath, and the present moment. Matthew Brensilver gently explores how hidden agendas, self-fixing, and projection can create tension, and he invites a more direct trust in awareness that is not transactional. The talk closes by widening practice into daily life, with an emphasis on tenderness, walking, and letting wisdom and love meet the human condition.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Settling into breath 01:18 Giving over to gravity 05:14 Hidden agendas 11:17 Nothing to fix 16:09 Nontransactional awareness 21:01 Letting reference points go 30:12 Falling into love 38:27 Love in daily life
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/86617/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relax into Alertness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on relaxing without losing clarity, beginning with a sense of sangha and settling into the body. He repeatedly reframes ease as a way of becoming more vivid, more attentive, and more genuinely alert, rather than drifting into dullness. The practice moves from balancing tiredness and arousal to resting the heart, noticing the mind’s states, and letting awareness do some of the work.
Chapters: 00:09 Entering Sangha 01:02 Balancing Energy 02:55 Relaxing for Clarity 04:58 Gentle Single-Pointing 08:33 Letting Go of Worry 12:25 Putting Down Busyness 17:00 Not Missing Goodness 20:22 Watching the Mind 22:37 Stability as Object
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21464

item.title: Guided Meditation: Gentleness & Equanimity
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on how gentleness and equanimity can work together: honest about what is here, and non-violent toward experience. The practice moves from settling into silence and breath, through softening the impulse to control, toward a deeper trust in allowing pleasant and unpleasant to come and go. Near the end, he points to a quiet contrast between clinging and love, and suggests that not trying to do anything with the mind may be the most gentle move of all.
Chapters: 00:12 Held by Silence 01:14 Radical Honesty 03:22 Not Fighting to Heal 07:03 Gentle Strength 10:03 Connecting, Not Controlling 14:41 Hands Soft, Clear 18:51 Knowing the Mind 21:40 Clinging and Love
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21412

item.title: Guided Meditation: Tranquility
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation oriented toward settling the body into tranquility without losing aliveness. The practice moves from posture and breath into the belly, then into working gently with hyper-arousal, urgency, and scattered attention. Later he invites resting with whatever pleasantness appears so that ease and tranquility can reinforce one another.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling in 01:20 Posture and gravity 02:58 Dropping to the belly 04:47 Softening the headspace 07:31 Working with arousal 10:38 Urgency and false alarms 15:58 Accountability without blame 21:46 Following pleasantness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21295

item.title: Guided Meditation: Concentration, Clarity, Equanimity
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation framed by gratitude for practice, then builds the session around three qualities of attention: concentration, clarity, and equanimity. He uses simple images and practical instructions to show how attention can stabilize, sharpen, and become less reactive to pleasure, pain, and change. The meditation gradually turns toward a more open stance: meeting each moment without landing on conclusions, reference points, or grasping.
Chapters: 00:12 Opening Gratitude 03:37 What Attention Needs 05:25 Higher-Resolution Clarity 06:54 Equanimity and Frictionlessness 13:17 Practice Moment by Moment 17:11 Rehearsing Freedom 21:36 Open-Ended Release
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21258
item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:

He uses simple images and practical instructions to show how attention can stabilize, sharpen, and become less reactive to pleasure, pain, and change. The meditation gradually turns toward a more open stance: meeting each moment without landing on conclusions, reference points, or grasping.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Effort as Not-Self
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on effort as a kind of not-self practice: instead of pushing harder, the instruction is to stop what can be stopped and receive the breath more openly. The talk explores how meditation can crystallize a sense of a separate doer, and then gently redirects attention toward a deeper, less anxious source of practice. After the instruction, the guidance settles into extended periods of silence punctuated by reminders to be seated and to avoid harsh, moralistic self-talk.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling and Stopping 00:43 Freedom Already Here 02:36 How Effort Becomes Self 05:06 Relaxing the Impulse 09:36 Receiving the Breath 14:15 Choosing the Right Counsel
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21242

item.title: Guided Meditation: Intimacy with Breathing
item.description:
This guided meditation stays close to the breath while exploring how attention can become more intimate with sensation. Matthew Brensilver invites listeners to notice the breath as fresh, immediate, and sensed through the whole body rather than held as an abstract object. The practice also makes room for tiredness, loose ends, and sticky emotions, returning again and again to breathing as a simple home base.
Chapters: 00:00 Arriving With Breath 00:56 Images and Sensation 03:25 Thin Slices of Time 06:10 Breath Awakens the Senses 07:45 Peace and Refuge 13:29 Forgiving the Mind 15:28 Home in the Breath 19:14 Sticky Thoughts to Breath
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21240

Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21240

item.title: Guided Meditation: Breath, Pleasure, Opening
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with a gentle invitation to soften the face and notice a simple, almost hidden smile. From there, Matthew Brensilver develops a practice of allowing pleasure to be present alongside difficulty, using the breath as a place to meet aliveness without forcing anything to disappear. The talk gradually widens into open awareness, then loosens even the instructions themselves so the practice can rest in sound, sensation, and trust.
Chapters: 00:10 Settling with a Smile 01:40 Joy with Difficulty 03:56 Relaxing and Receiving 08:13 Breath and Pleasure 12:57 An Anchor Everywhere 14:45 Opening to Awareness 20:41 Letting Instructions Go
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21233

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not Predicting Me
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around softening the habit of predicting who we are from one moment to the next. He points to the “song of self” as something that can be noticed rather than believed, and uses that noticing to make breathing and body awareness feel less crowded. The practice gradually turns toward a quieter, more global wakefulness in which experience is allowed to be wider than any fixed story.
Chapters: 00:00 Arrival 01:00 Predicting Ourselves 02:57 Opening to Not-Knowing 06:05 The Song of Self 10:46 Love It to Death 12:59 Settling Into Breath 25:21 Global Wakefulness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21224

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing into the Moment
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on relaxing into the moment with honesty rather than trying to fix experience. The practice moves from settling the breath and body to meeting thoughts and worries with less struggle, then widens into a steadier sense of refuge and trust. By the end, the emphasis is on honoring the moment as a form of healing in itself.
Chapters: 00:12 Honesty With the Moment 01:04 Settling With Breath 03:31 Relaxing the Problem Habit 06:24 Body as Refuge 08:56 Soothing and Energizing 12:00 Dissolving Worry 17:07 Finding Refuge 25:35 What Truly Matters
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21219

Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21219

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing into Love
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by orienting the body toward ease without losing wakefulness, then moves into breath, heart center, and a softening toward love. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the idea that when the mind and body settle, awareness can take on the hue of kindness, and he invites a willingness to feel whatever is present—joy, sorrow, pain, or tenderness—without contracting around it. The practice widens into phrases such as “May I be well” and “May all beings be well,” ending in a quiet emphasis on non-violence, refuge, and benevolent wish.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling and posture 01:25 Breath as softening 03:41 Awareness with love 05:32 Heart posture 10:09 Bathing pain in care 14:49 Lovingkindness phrases
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21198

item.title: Saturday Guided Sit
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided sit centered on feeling-tone: noticing the body, relaxing the impulse to do more, and sensing how arousal and valence shape experience. He repeatedly returns to receiving experience rather than building an action plan from it, and later connects that to the Buddha’s teaching that all dhammas converge on feeling. The closing widens into practical retreat advice about silence, habit, and staying close to practice as the day unfolds.
Chapters: 00:01 Settling the posture 04:04 Whole-body awareness 10:15 Arousal and valence 13:47 Clarifying feeling 18:13 Receiving without counsel 24:57 The self-story loosens 29:51 Feeling as practice 41:33 Retreat and closing notes
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21175

item.title: Thursday Guided Meditation
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by normalizing fear, bracing, and doubt, inviting them to be met with kindness rather than treated as a problem. It then settles into breath as an anchor, widening into the body as a whole, and later turns toward the way attention drifts into memory, anticipation, and the impulse to fix things. The practice closes with a softer sense of surrender and a hint of how intentions can line up when effort becomes less forceful.
Chapters: 00:01 Arriving with fear 02:56 Common practice difficulties 04:37 Finding the breath 09:13 Breath through the body 14:06 Willingness and curiosity 21:51 Grounded openness 28:02 Memory and anticipation 37:36 Letting intentions align
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21146

item.title: Formal Opening Part 1 (sit)
item.description:
A brief opening for retreat, centered on beginning with a 15-minute sit and then moving through logistics for the days ahead. Matthew Brensilver covers seating, hearing assistance, the basic alternation of sitting and walking meditation, and the schedule for instruction, talks, reflections, and discussion groups. The talk ends by naming the value of everyone having set aside time for retreat.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the retreat 15:10 Comfort and seating 16:32 Daily meditation rhythm 16:58 Instruction and talks 17:08 Practice groups 17:35 Bell practice 18:05 Closing encouragement
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21138

item.title: Guided Meditation: Feeling and Verbal Thinking
item.description:
This guided meditation opens by questioning the impulse to reach for technique and inviting a more undefended, surrendering posture. From there, Matthew Brensilver leads attention into bodily feeling, emotional charge, and the way inner speech can be heard as sound rather than obeyed automatically. The meditation gradually widens into a quiet, patient awareness of the relationship between feelings and words, ending with an emphasis on allowing experience to unfold with blessing and equanimity.
Chapters: 00:00 Arriving and Settling 02:40 Letting Go of Technique 05:44 Breath and Release 07:45 Feeling in the Body 11:02 Patience and Permission 14:19 Hearing Thoughts 19:18 Feelings and Stories 23:56 Equanimity and Blessing
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21104

item.title: Guided Meditation: You cannot know oneself while fixing oneself
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on resting rather than fixing, beginning with posture, grounding, and the breath as a way of settling the body-mind. The practice gradually opens into a quiet inquiry into samsara, fear, and the urge to make meaning, with repeated reminders that what arises can be met without willfulness. Near the end, the instruction turns toward simple attentiveness: silence as anchor, and even noise met with infinite patience.
Chapters: 00:36 Settling the body 03:59 Breath and ease 05:58 Putting down the project 09:14 Simple, beckoning practice 15:41 Phenomena and fear 17:23 Wisp-like experience 23:28 Silence and patience
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21063

Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21063

item.title: Guided Meditation: Pervasiveness of Craving
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation on craving, control, and the tendency to shape experience. The practice moves from settling the body into a posture of surrender, to noticing the tension of holding on, to softening around heartache and the wish for life to be different. He repeatedly returns to the possibility that awareness can be trusted, and that this moment can be received as a source of wisdom rather than a problem to solve.
Chapters: 00:56 Settling Into Posture 03:21 Noticing Holding On 05:59 Surrendering to This Moment 09:03 Breath as Pleasure 13:12 Responsibility Without Power 15:30 Trusting Awareness 20:21 Receiving Wisdom
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20987

Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20987

item.title: Guided Meditation: Too Busy Knowing
item.description:
A guided meditation built around relaxing vigilance and trusting the body’s capacity to meet experience. Matthew Brensilver invites a quieter mode of attention: less patrol, more surrender, while keeping the mind clear and awake. The practice returns several times to impermanence and to the felt sense of experience changing moment by moment, without freezing it into something solid.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling and Relaxing 04:10 Safety and Relinquishment 06:56 Anchors and Clarity 09:23 Snagged by Experience 10:07 Moment by Moment Knowing 20:58 Too Busy Knowing
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20921

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sitting in the Bones
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on settling attention into the body, especially the felt sense of the bones, heaviness, and gravity. The practice moves from fuller breathing and relaxation into a steadier, mountain-like groundedness, then broadens into the freedom to let experience arise and pass without strain. In the closing, he brings in loving-kindness as a quiet ethical and emotional dimension of the practice.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling the Breath 02:30 Sitting in the Bones 04:11 Gravity and Earth 06:01 Attention Downward 09:34 Mountain Stability 11:01 Stop Doing So Much 17:28 The Self Can Relax 23:11 From Rest to Metta
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20864

item.title: Guided Meditation: Landing in the Breath and Soundscape
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation and retreat orientation that begins with fear, softness, and the need to feel safe enough to simplify attention. He then moves through breath practice, soundscape awareness, and walking meditation, returning again and again to the idea that practice is less about willpower than willingness. Near the end, he frames boredom, overwhelm, and even ordinary retreat difficulty as part of the field to be known with gentleness.
Location: Big Bear Retreat Center
Chapters: 00:01 Settling fear 04:29 Mood in awareness 10:03 Breath as refuge 17:02 Thinking without struggle 24:06 Awake and relaxed 29:59 Listening to soundscape 37:50 Retreat rhythm 45:25 Whole-day awareness 52:57 Closing encouragement
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/85473/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Non-tension
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on non-tension: noticing how the body and mind respond to conditions, and learning not to wait for things to be perfect before letting go. The practice moves from settling into bodily awareness, to relaxing clinging and bracing, to resting with impermanence without pushing or pulling against it. The closing section uses a repeated image of falling into the present, with less baggage and fewer places for attention to snag.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and welcome 01:45 Temperature and the body 04:13 Not waiting for perfect 12:05 Awareness in the body 14:54 Relaxing clinging 18:52 Impermanence as practice 23:28 Falling into presence
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20758

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing and Falling Awake
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on settling the body and mind without forcing ease. The practice moves from posture and releasing the day, through soft attention to breath and sound, toward a gentler understanding of relaxation as relinquishment rather than control. He returns repeatedly to the idea that tension is bound up with grasping, and that waking up may happen in a way closer to falling asleep than to trying harder.
Chapters: 00:29 Settling Into Posture 01:51 Laying Down the Day 04:19 Blessing and Release 06:17 Breath and Sound 11:05 Tension and Holding 15:37 Relinquishing Control 21:07 Relaxing as Exhale 24:30 Falling Awake
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20693

Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20693

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxation and the Tension of Dukkha, Self, Clinging
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that uses relaxation as a way to notice the subtle tension bound up with clinging, selfing, greed, hatred, and delusion. He moves from an image of a sleepy child asking for comfort to a quieter investigation of how deep release can reveal what the body-mind has been holding. The practice is gentle but pointed: soften, breathe, let gravity help, and keep putting things down until awareness feels less burdened.
Chapters: 00:14 Opening story 01:43 Relaxation and suffering 05:36 Right here to sit 07:23 Let tension speak 10:21 Gravity and ease 13:35 Self as contraction 18:28 Clinging and hate 21:42 Delusion and mortality 24:15 Putting things down
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20627

item.title: Guided Meditation: Self and Willfulness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation that keeps returning to a simple question: what happens if we stop trying to make meditation happen? He gently examines how willfulness can create a self that manages experience, and invites a softer trust in awareness, breath, body, and sound. The practice gradually shifts from effort and suspicion toward surrender, letting doingness dissolve into openness.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 01:00 What Began Meditation 04:07 Trusting the Mind 06:31 The Self as Organizer 08:09 Anchor Everywhere 12:17 Willfulness and Job 15:25 Awareness Finds Us 21:44 Surrender and Ease
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20585

Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20585

item.title: Guided Meditation: Tranquility
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on tranquility as both settling and clearer seeing. He begins with breath, bodily ease, and releasing clinging, then widens the practice to listening to silence, resting in the visual field, and allowing attention to soften around experience. A brief reference to Mother Teresa’s description of prayer helps frame the practice as a quiet, receptive listening, without requiring any religious belief.
Chapters: 00:09 Tranquility and clarity 01:56 Resting the body 05:18 Softening clinging 07:28 Body as a resource 10:54 Listening for silence 14:59 Silent listening 17:37 Visual field and darkness 23:27 Letting clinging wait 25:50 Closing rest
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20530

item.title: Guided Meditation: Goodnight Moon
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around the rhythm of Goodnight Moon, using the book’s repeated bedtime refrain as a gentle model for letting experience rest. He invites listeners to say goodnight to memories, pain, thoughts, and feelings, then to settle into breathing and open awareness without forcing anything away. The practice gradually widens from soft release into a quiet sense of presence, effortlessness, and brightness.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the Meditation 02:36 Saying Goodnight 09:15 Settling With Breath 11:49 Open Awareness 15:04 Not Rejection 16:08 Resting in Presence 26:02 Fatigue and Brightness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20468

item.title: Guided Meditation: Feeling
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation centered on feeling, beginning with breath and a gentle turn away from compulsive problem-solving. The practice moves through the affective roots of reactivity, the ways feeling gets turned into story, and the invitation to let experience unwind without feeding it with thought. Later he broadens into retreat-specific reflection on futurelessness, death, effort, boredom, and how to keep practice steady without turning effort into self-strain.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Settling With Breath 02:14 Problems and Dissolving 03:10 Commitment To This Moment 06:25 Wanting And Knowing 09:46 Feeling And Reactivity 13:46 Stories From Feeling 20:12 Non-Thinking And Release 23:11 Clinging And Heaven 28:35 Futurelessness And Death
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/83891/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Convergence of Insight and Love (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver frames the talk around the convergence of insight and love: mindfulness as a careful deconstruction of experience, and the brahma-viharas as the heart’s place to rest. He moves through the four “species of love” — loving-kindness, compassion, sympathetic joy, and equanimity — showing how each meets a different kind of life moment, from goodness to suffering to helplessness. The guided portion then turns inward, inviting listeners to stop measuring, sense what is needed, and offer phrases of goodwill that soften the heart rather than harden it.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Insight and love 05:13 The four abodes 08:29 Compassion and equanimity 12:40 Letting love be home 14:31 Dropping specialness 22:44 Offering goodwill 27:55 Softening into the heart
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/83872/

item.title: Guided Meditation: The Breath and Awareness don't depend on you
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation that begins with settling the body and reframing the breath as something that does not depend on the usual sense of “me.” From there he turns toward awareness itself, emphasizing that it also is not owned or controlled, and uses the language of emptiness to soften selfing. The back half of the talk lingers in long repetitions and then opens into an image of egoic “proprioception,” showing how a sense of self is continually maintained until awareness becomes less identified and more spacious.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 01:15 Breath Beyond Self 03:27 Awareness Receives 05:22 Emptiness Repeated 11:07 Ego's Bureaucracy 13:08 Same Stuff, Rising 17:42 Egoic Proprioception 20:22 Less and Less Me
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20353

item.title: Guided Meditation: Awareness and Effortlessness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores the shift from narrating experience to resting in awareness itself, drawing on Thomas Metzinger to describe silence as non-conceptual, non-reactive knowing. The talk moves from theory into guided sitting, where effort, choicelessness, and relaxation are treated as supports that can eventually fall away. Later he turns toward equanimity, frictionlessness, and the sense that the moment is no longer a problem to solve but something to trust.
Chapters: 00:30 Narrative Self 02:23 Silent Knowing 05:07 Instructions And Fruit 07:18 Settling To Sit 09:17 Friction And Equanimity 12:32 Effort Toward Release 18:19 Trusting Silence 25:26 Resting Safely
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20281

item.title: Guided Meditation: Language as Safety
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation framed around a gentle critique of our reliance on language for comfort and control. The practice moves from settling the body and allowing imperfection, into resting with feeling and breath without immediately turning experience into a story. Near the end, he names the felt contrast directly: words can seem like safety, but deeper safety comes from wisdom, love, and learning to stay with uncertainty.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening reflection 02:09 Settling the body 05:18 Releasing urgency 09:07 Rest without words 10:27 Breath and feeling 14:39 Uncertainty and story 19:32 Letting go into silence 25:34 Risk and deeper safety
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20239

item.title: Guided Meditation: Attention, Effort, Choice and Selfing
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation framed around a subtle paradox: meditation asks for attention, effort, and choice, while also pointing toward choicelessness, effortlessness, and surrender. He keeps returning to the way practice can strengthen a sense of doer and meditator, then gently loosens that grip by treating selfing as something to study rather than defeat. The guidance moves from settling posture and narrowing attention to breathing, through reminders and rebalancing, and toward a quieter release of the need to manage the meditation at all.
Chapters: 00:12 Opening orientation 00:42 Attention and effort 02:26 Selfing and practice 06:12 Settling with breath 09:03 Stability and reminders 14:33 Doer and deed 19:33 Surrender to grace 24:40 Beyond hierarchy
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20193

item.title: Guided Meditation: Aperture, Vividness, Stability, Effort
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around four practical dimensions of practice: intention, attention, vividness, stability, and effort. He repeatedly returns to the sense that meditation is not a workspace for solving problems, but a period of putting down grasping and letting the moment be enough. The sit then moves from a more concrete anchor to a broader, more open awareness, with guidance on how to adjust effort without strain.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening frame 01:19 Intention to release 02:28 Attention and aperture 04:34 Vividness and stability 06:01 Effort in balance 07:42 Settling into the sit 12:40 Anchor and soundscape 17:17 Returning from mind wandering 26:46 Open awareness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20159

item.title: Guided Meditation: What's Already Here?
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens with a brief reflection on war, cooperation, and the idea that peace may be something human beings have to invent. He then turns the talk into a guided meditation centered on a recurring instruction: stop looking, and let awareness, breath, silence, and even healing "find" you. The practice keeps returning to the ordinary fact that what we seek is already present, and that freedom and suffering meet in the same place.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening reflection 02:25 Freedom in suffering 04:13 Settling the body 06:13 Letting breath arrive 10:39 Being found 17:10 Resting in presence 22:44 Healing the search
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20105

item.title: Guided Meditation: Faith in Silence
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on learning to trust silence instead of using thought as a constant coping strategy. He gently argues that the body’s signals, the mind’s reactions, and the urge to solve everything do not need to be read as threats, and he repeatedly returns to the reassurance that it is okay to let things rest. The practice moves from settling the posture and soothing the body into longer stretches of silence, with reminders to soften, stop over-reading experience, and listen for what tranquility makes possible.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling In 02:41 Soothing the Body 05:06 Putting Down Complications 10:18 Faith in Silence 15:52 Over-Reading Experience 18:48 Heart Training 20:05 Listening To Silence 25:20 Extended Quiet
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20005

item.title: Guided Meditation: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation that keeps returning to receptive listening: allowing experience to be received before it hardens into resistance or identification. The talk moves from settling the body, to noticing the quiet agenda of “me” in meditation, to softening around wanting, control, and the effort to make something happen. Near the end, he frames practice in terms of faith — the possibility that something can hold us even when control is released.
Chapters: 00:30 Settling In 01:30 Deep Listening 05:52 The Project of Me 10:01 Wanting and Agenda 14:12 Trusting Awareness 21:20 Control or Connection 25:47 Faith in Letting Go
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19999

item.title: Guided Meditation: Attention as Prayer
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver frames this guided meditation around Simone Weil’s line that attention at its highest degree is prayer, then unfolds that idea through body, breath, and sound. The practice moves from a somewhat deliberate, instructed beginning toward a softer sense that awareness is not owned or controlled by a separate meditator. Along the way he weaves in forgiveness, devotion, and the gradual loosening of selfing until attention feels more like surrender than effort.
Chapters: 01:05 Attention as Prayer 02:53 View and Devotion 04:53 Settling the Body 07:38 Forgiveness and Recommitment 10:44 Love in Disguise 14:11 Less Control, More Grace 17:56 Letting Awareness Find Us 26:12 Groundless and Held
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19984

item.title: Guided Meditation: Attention and Sustaining Openness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on two linked skills: training attention and sustaining openness when intensity arises. The talk repeatedly returns to how awareness narrows into problem-solving and how practice can gently re-open the field, with attention to the body, breath, soundscape, and a sense of okayness. Rather than building a conceptual map, it keeps pointing back to direct experience, patience, and relinquishment as ways to meet ordinary and subtle stress.
Chapters: 00:00 Practice frame 02:50 Settle into posture 05:13 Intensity and collapse 09:38 Reverence for dukkha 13:43 Good enough awareness 19:47 Re-establish openness 26:31 Not solving
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19970

item.title: Guided Meditation: Establishing Tranquility
item.description:
This guided meditation settles into posture, then turns attention toward silence as a quieter guide than the mind's usual instructions. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly narrows the attentional field to the breath, inviting the listener to relax what can be relaxed, allow what remains, and feel how even a small measure of tranquility changes the way experience appears. Near the end, he connects that shift to a Buddhist teaching that mind conditions what is known, closing with a gentle emphasis on refuge, nourishment, and a softer inner gaze.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling Into Silence 02:01 Relaxing the Body 03:00 Tranquility Changes View 06:22 Narrowing Attention 11:22 Faith in Silence 20:52 Stabilizing the Inner Gaze 26:04 Tranquility as Refuge 28:22 Closing Bells
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19960

item.title: Guided Meditation - Deep Letting Go
item.description:
This guided meditation explores a deep kind of letting go by loosening the sense of being stuck in time, self-story, and reactive wanting. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly invites the listener to rest outside memory and possibility, and to notice how clinging helps create the very sense of time it seems to live within. The practice moves gradually from a question of heart posture into spaciousness, emptiness, and a quieter encounter with experience.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening question 00:30 Letting go inwardly 03:56 Outside memory and future 07:04 Time and clinging 11:09 Dropping reference points 15:09 Becoming empty 21:59 Hope and fear soften 32:17 Putting down self-models
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19756

item.title: Guided Meditation - Mindfulness of Feeling
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on mindfulness of feeling, beginning with rain, body awareness, and the simple instruction to let go. He then turns toward the Buddha’s teaching that all dharma converge on feeling, using it to distinguish raw feeling from the stories, urgencies, and self-judgments that can grow around it. The practice moves gently from soothing and breathing with sensation toward equanimity, listening, and a more loving relationship to whatever arises.
Chapters: 01:00 Settling with Rain 02:47 Letting Go 06:32 Feeling as Dharma 08:20 Body and Affect 11:34 The Inner Meter 14:35 Equanimity and Soothing 20:35 Anchors and Urgency 29:51 Listening Instead of Reacting 34:30 Feeling into Love
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19662

item.title: Guided Meditation - Landing in the Breath
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that moves from settling fear and bracing into a close, steady contact with breathing. He emphasizes that discomfort, thought, and uncertainty can be met as present experience rather than warnings about the future, and gradually opens into walking meditation and the continuity of mindfulness across posture changes. The talk closes by normalizing the ordinary difficulties of retreat and framing surrender, simplicity, and patience as part of the practice itself.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the Heart 02:23 Thoughts as Predictions 06:21 Body Aliveness 12:38 Finding the Breath 19:38 Returning Without Judgment 26:47 Retreat Mind States 40:54 The Obstacle Is Path 47:23 Walking and Momentum 54:37 Surrender and Simplicity
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19649

item.title: Guided Meditation: Resting In Care
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on resting in care, beginning by inviting a felt sense of love, gratitude, compassion, or even grief as a doorway into the heart. From there he explores how care can color the whole body and make each breath feel significant, then turns toward the freedom that comes when love is steady enough to hold what is not love without forcing anything. The practice closes with a gentle wish for release from suffering.
Chapters: 01:06 Settling In 02:43 Begin With Love 07:25 Care Flavors Experience 09:39 Body Suffused With Care 13:45 Freedom Still Beckons 16:25 Steadier Love 22:04 Holding What Is Not Love 23:23 Closing Wish
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19441

item.title: Guided Meditation: Resting In What We Cannot Stop
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on resting in awareness as something that is already present and not fully under personal control. The talk moves from settling the body and appreciating being alive, into a careful distinction between the effort of practice and the effortlessness of awareness itself. He repeatedly points back to the phrase that whatever cannot be stopped is where to rest, treating mindfulness as a kind of grace rather than a project of will.
Chapters: 00:52 Settling In 04:00 Gratitude for Awareness 09:32 Effort and Ease 11:37 Awareness Beyond Will 20:23 Mindfulness as Grace 26:15 Resting in What Cannot Stop
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19425

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting the Moment Soften You
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on the felt sense of softening rather than forcing concentration. He moves through breath, body, thought, emotion, and sound as different ways the moment can be met, with repeated encouragement to meet experience with blessing, awareness, and release. The practice ends by framing this softening as rehearsal for freedom: not pretending discomfort is pleasant, but learning to let go again and again.
Chapters: 00:12 Arriving 01:13 Entering the moment 02:20 Finding the breath 03:39 Meeting the body 09:06 Softened by phenomena 11:17 When suppleness is lost 18:18 Letting go in practice 26:02 Rehearsing freedom
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19422

item.title: Guided Meditation: Renouncing the Narrative Impulse
item.description:
This guided meditation invites steady attention into bodily feeling and the tendency to turn sensation into a story. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes renouncing the narrative impulse—not by suppressing thought, but by meeting feeling as feeling and softening the reflex to search for meaning, threat, or command. The practice gradually opens toward defenselessness, spaciousness, and the possibility that nothing needs to happen right now.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and Welcome 00:50 Inviting Goodwill 03:13 Landing in the Body 06:56 Feeling Without Story 10:23 Narrative and Vigilance 16:18 Experimenting with Defenselessness 20:50 Nothing Needs to Happen 27:46 Distilling Wisdom
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19406

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not updating your autobiography
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation and reflection on not turning each moment into a continuation of the personal story. He repeatedly returns to the body, breath, and sensory precision, then widens into equanimity, non-clinging, and a kind of futurelessness that lets this moment be enough. The talk moves gently between instruction and reflection, emphasizing surrender without erasing memory or life history.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling In 02:37 Body and Breath 06:19 Awareness Spreads 10:20 Not the Next Chapter 12:09 Letting Go of Story 19:07 Equanimity and Non-clinging 21:17 Self as Hologram 26:58 Futurelessness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19397

item.title: Guided Meditation - The Warmth of Love
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on warmth as a lived quality rather than a metaphor, moving gently from settling the body into the wider field of love, compassion, joy, and equanimity. The guidance emphasizes allowing, forgiving, and making a truce with imperfection, then opens into phrases of well-wishing and a quiet acceptance of what cannot be controlled. A brief closing chant may be present near the end, though its exact framing is not fully clear.
Chapters: 00:01 Settling In 02:45 Warmth of Love 11:11 Allowing and Letting Go 24:49 What the Heart Needs 26:27 Metta and Compassion 28:24 Equanimity at Limits 30:01 Well-Wishing
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19368

item.title: Guided Meditation - Love and Grief
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by settling the body and then opens into a reflection on Leonard Cohen’s “Treaty,” using the song’s longing for peace as a way into the meeting of love and grief. Matthew then explores grief as something that may belong within practice, even suggesting it could be understood as a kind of fifth Brahma-vihara, before turning to impermanence and how grief can mature into compassion, gratitude, and reverence. The session closes with metta phrases and a final emphasis on how intimate contact with our own suffering can deepen our response to others.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the Body 07:40 Leonard Cohen's Treaty 09:09 Starting the Practice 11:42 Love and Grief 15:17 Grieving Impermanence 16:33 Opening Into Metta 21:23 Wishing Well 23:05 Compassion for Others
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19371

item.title: Friday Morning Instructions
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores how perception is shaped by motivation, then turns toward letting go as a way of seeing more clearly. He moves from the innocence of clinging and the body’s affective patterns into a steadier, more compassionate relationship with experience, including the repeated refrain that we are not doing our life wrong. The talk closes on trust: awareness is not a story, and when we fall, love may be what catches us.
Chapters: 00:01 Wanting Shapes Seeing 01:37 Letting Go 03:14 Clinging and Self-Knowledge 09:21 Affect and Innocence 17:52 Suffering and Delusion 20:42 Mind In Images 27:43 Awareness Is Not Story 29:50 Faith and Being Held
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19240

item.title: Wednesday morning instructions
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet set of instructions for sitting with experience as something moving and changing rather than fixed. He keeps returning to posture, nervous system, and the practice of meeting feeling with awareness, patience, and sensitivity, then opens that ground into equanimity, love, and a frank view of the human condition. The talk includes a few clearly garbled transcript passages; the teaching itself remains steady and coherent.
Chapters: 00:01 Changing experience 03:19 Returning to the body 09:03 Thought as aftermath 16:14 Soothing the system 24:52 Being changed 29:12 Equanimity’s by-products 31:38 The necessity of love 44:01 Awareness and helplessness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19237

item.title: Morning Instructions: Landing in the body breathing
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens this first guided sit of a week-long retreat by inviting listeners to set down the background project of self-management and settle into the body. The practice unfolds as a steady return to softening—face, shoulders, belly, and breath—along with a gentler relationship to attention, fatigue, and restlessness. In the second half, he widens the frame to walking practice, continuity through the day, and the patience to keep practicing when nothing seems to be happening.
Chapters: 00:01 Arriving at retreat 04:53 Soften the face 10:21 Let breath emerge 17:27 Interest over force 26:30 Restlessness and fatigue 32:59 Trust the day 43:09 Walking practice 50:31 Boredom and trust
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19232

item.title: Reflections and Guided Meditation on Mudita
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver reflects on mudita, or appreciative joy, by first grounding it in tenderness, grief, and the ways the heart can stay open without pretending. He then turns to comparison and envy as forms of suffering that support a false self, and shows how practice can loosen that grip through compassion, softness, and rejoicing in another’s welfare. The talk closes with a guided meditation that begins with an easy person, then moves toward someone who stirs comparison, and ends in self-care and blessing.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Opening reflection 04:00 Tears and tenderness 06:19 Joy without pretending 08:54 What mudita is 12:15 Selfing through comparison 15:40 Sharing another’s good 20:31 Guided practice begins 24:53 Working with envy 29:07 Softening and closing
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/80555/

item.title: Guided meditation: all phenomena as false alarms
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that keeps returning to a simple instruction: stop the extra doing, and meet experience with care. He frames sounds, thoughts, body sensations, and emotional surges as “false alarms,” inviting listeners not to turn every feeling into a story about the self. The talk gradually widens into a quiet teaching on confidence, emptiness, and love: if phenomena are not owned, they can soften the heart rather than harm it.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the Doing 03:19 Care for the Heart 05:54 False Alarms 10:12 Confidence and Softening 14:54 Emotion and Urgency 21:06 No Story Needed 32:28 Love After Selfing 40:51 Questions and Closing Guidance
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/80546/

item.title: Metta Practice
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver presents metta as something more than a pleasant mood: a capacity that makes insight practice bearable, especially when the heart meets the full intensity of suffering. He moves through the four brahmaviharas as four distinct kinds of love, then explains how metta can cultivate warmth, steady the mind, or bring buried grief and fear to the surface for healing. The talk closes with a simple practice of repeating phrases, listening for their effect, and widening the field of care to others without denying war, oppression, or loss.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Love and Insight 02:28 Four Species of Love 06:33 Why Love Matters 10:56 Meeting What Hurts 12:45 Settling the Body 16:55 Remembering Goodness 25:32 Abiding in Kindness 31:33 Extending the Wish 37:04 A Sublime Abiding
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/80506/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Deconstructing Sensory Experience
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around a simple three-part discernment: seeing, hearing, and feeling. After introducing the method and naming the tendency to turn practice into self-improvement, he leads a gradual scanning of visual thought, inner speech, and affect in the body, with repeated emphasis on noticing arising and passing without collapsing into identification. The practice closes by cycling through the three sensory spheres and resting in the untangling of “I am-ness.”
Chapters: 00:12 Opening the method 01:43 Seeing, hearing, feeling 05:50 Settling and surrendering 08:28 Noticing visual thought 11:38 Listening to inner speech 17:25 Feeling in the body 19:52 Opening to all three 24:16 Cycling and untangling
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/18180

item.title: Guided Meditation: Awareness Without Willfulness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that keeps returning to the difference between effort and willfulness. After a brief, candid opening about being emotionally caught up in a basketball game, he settles into posture, body, and breath, then invites a steady awareness of the residues of the day without trying to fix them. The practice gradually shifts toward relaxing the impulse to do something to experience, resting instead in an awareness that feels less personal and less effortful.
Chapters: 00:35 Opening candor 02:29 Settle the body 04:40 Taking stock 07:03 Allowing residue 07:59 Breath and balance 11:41 Effort and grace 17:19 Relaxing the doer 22:51 Awareness without self 30:03 Closing bells
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/18167

item.title: Guided Meditation: Listening to the Mind of Buddha
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that gradually shifts from settling into the room toward a practice of listening: first to breath, body, sound, and the darkness behind closed eyes, then to a deeper receptivity he links to Leonard Cohen’s hummingbird lines. The talk emphasizes trusting awareness, letting silence beckon, and resting without over-managing experience. It closes by naming practice as a way to stop endlessly coping and simply be awake.
Chapters: 00:11 Arriving and Settling 01:30 Silence Beckons 02:58 Simple Objects of Attention 05:04 Listening to Cohen 06:23 Trusting Awareness 15:23 Forgiveness and Rest 17:33 Patience and Urgency 23:35 Beyond Coping
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/18176

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not Locating Oneself
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation organized around not re-establishing the usual sense of self. He invites a gentle stance of not knowing, then balances attention to the meditation object with attention to how attention is being held. The talk gradually settles into a quiet instruction to let self-references dissolve into the openness of the present moment.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and welcome 00:30 Settling without knowing 01:20 Not locating a self 03:30 Opening practice 08:09 What and how 09:36 Sustained guidance 18:29 Care and openness 22:10 Closing silence
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/18177

item.title: Guided Meditation: Entering the Dharma Field
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a gentle guided meditation that begins by settling into posture and letting the breath remind us of the Dharma. The practice moves from opening to the intensity of life, to finding refuge in breathing, and finally to entering a wider "Dharma field" where even mistakes can become part of the path. Near the end, he draws on Ajahn Sucitto’s language to reframe the meditation as stepping into a realm that continually recalls Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha.
Chapters: 00:42 Arriving and settling 04:52 Breath as reminder 07:26 Opening and refuge 09:20 Breath as shelter 14:21 Folding thoughts back 18:51 Hope and fear 21:51 Invoking the Buddha 25:06 Entering Dharma field
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/18164

item.title: Guided Meditation: Trajectory of your life and trajectory of freedom
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver frames the meditation around two trajectories: the anxious, habit-driven story of a life, and the quieter training that leads toward freedom. The talk moves from this reflection into guided practice, emphasizing relaxation, sensing the whole body, and meeting feeling states without treating them as evidence that something is wrong. The practice repeatedly returns to softness, awareness, and the possibility that you do not have to become anyone in order to be present.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Two Trajectories 06:09 Letting Go 13:25 Settling Into Practice 18:11 Undefended Presence 23:50 False Alarms 29:36 Softening the Heart
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/77006/

item.title: Moved by goodness: Reflections and Guided Metta Meditation (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver begins by contrasting mindfulness as deconstruction with metta as a rebuilding of the heart out of love. He then sketches loving-kindness as a boundless, noncontrolling form of love that is deepened by grief, and he places it within the four divine abodes. The talk moves into a guided metta meditation, first toward self-acceptance and then toward a quieter, gentler blessing practice that treats words as a bell for the body to hear.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Deconstruction and love 02:17 What love means here 05:28 The four divine abodes 09:43 Moved by goodness 12:35 Cultivation and purification 17:12 Settling into practice 25:15 Self-blessing phrases 31:40 Expansiveness of love
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/77000/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not reading the tea leaves of the body
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on meeting body experience without turning it into a puzzle to solve. He repeatedly returns to the idea that sensations, emotions, and alarms can be received as data, then gently released, rather than interpreted as signs of what will happen next. Near the end, he brings in the Mula Sutta to frame each experience as a doorway to Dharma, with liberation rather than self-analysis as the orientation.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening into welcome 02:43 Receiving the body 05:09 Not solving problems 07:16 Past and future pressure 14:38 False alarms 17:01 This moment only 23:31 Letting go repeatedly 28:53 Liberation in the sutta 33:44 Renouncing words
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17989

item.title: Guided Meditation: Settling and Opening
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with a very simple orientation to posture, embodied awareness, and relaxation, then gradually widens into advice for retreat practice. He repeatedly returns to the ways fear, hope, fatigue, and agitation shape the mind, and encourages listeners not to over-interpret what arises on the first day. The talk ends by extending mindfulness into walking practice and daily continuity, with a gentle reminder that one difficult day is not a basis for conclusions.
Chapters: 00:00 Simple settling 02:00 Fear and interpretation 07:25 Breath as refuge 18:06 Questions dissolve 23:35 Cycles of breath 33:37 Retreat opens the heart 38:20 Meeting what arises 41:20 Walking with continuity 51:53 No conclusions today
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17986

item.title: Guided Meditation: Stability and Change
item.description:
This guided meditation settles into the body, the breath, and the felt support of the earth before opening into a reflection on stability and change. Matthew Brensilver gradually shifts from simple instructions to a quieter contemplation of impermanence, asking what it means to rest in awareness without trying to hold experience still. The talk ends by widening into a river image: life as continuous flow, with brief moments of solidifying and release.
Chapters: 00:41 Settling Together 02:11 Resting In Awareness 04:42 Gravity And Breath 08:41 Everything In Motion 10:27 Refuge And Center 13:12 Flexible Love 17:10 River Of Life 23:35 Open Mesh Of Being
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17880

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sensitivity all the way down
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on receptivity, using chitta as a way of understanding the heart-mind as something inherently open and affected. The practice moves from settling the body, to recognizing how we brace against pain and self-protection, and then toward a steadier openness that does not need to land in conclusions. Near the end, he emphasizes that goodness is already here, and that the Dharma can be trusted through direct feeling rather than striving.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 01:22 Chitta as Receptivity 03:22 Porous and Poised 05:28 Always Being Touched 10:00 False Alarms 16:40 Bracing and Protection 18:57 Openness Without Defense 24:50 Goodness Already Here
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17755

item.title: Guided Meditation: Openness of Awareness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on how attention shapes experience. He moves from settling the body and softening vigilance into breath-based practices that brighten, soothe, and release, then opens into a wider sense of awareness. The talk closes by contrasting that grounded simplicity with the pull of hope and fear, and how the heart can see things differently when rest and seclusion are present.
Chapters: 00:12 Opening Welcome 00:51 Attention Shapes Experience 01:50 Settling the Body 05:34 Breath as Practice 09:07 Release and Suspension 13:15 Seclusion in Breath 19:57 Hope and Fear 23:57 Closing Reflection
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17753

item.title: Guided Meditation: Thought as the exhaust from engine of affect
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around attunement to present-moment experience, especially the way feeling gives rise to interpretation and mental proliferation. He invites listeners to notice the body’s surges without over-reading them, then repeatedly returns to a single instruction: soften. The practice gradually widens into resting with unpleasantness, letting the Dharma saturate experience, and allowing felt urgency to be understood more gently.
Chapters: 00:12 Arriving Together 01:41 Attunement and Empathy 05:21 Feeling Becomes Meaning 08:55 Reading the Body 11:26 Not Over-Interpreting 14:04 Soften 20:29 Resting in Unpleasantness 23:01 Keeping Connection
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17734

item.title: Guided Meditation: Entering the Dharma
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on “entering the Dharma” as a bodily, felt act rather than a project of self-improvement. The practice moves through settling the body, recollecting Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha, and opening to a softer stance of receptivity and surrender. Thich Nhat Hanh’s image of the nectar settling in a glass becomes a recurring touchstone for letting experience clarify on its own.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 01:40 Entering the Dharma 04:06 Nectar Settles 07:13 Recollecting Refuge 09:38 Breath and Blessing 11:04 Receptive Attention 16:30 Surrender and Acceptance 23:36 Trusting Openness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17743

item.title: Guided Meditation: Surrendering to the Present
item.description:
In this guided meditation, Matthew Brensilver invites listeners to settle the body, relax the mind, and let awareness gently include whatever is currently unresolved. The practice turns toward the difficulty of clinging and control, suggesting that presence involves making enough peace with experience that attention is no longer pulled away by it. From there, the talk opens into a quieter letting go: hopes and fears remain, but they become less entangling, and the heart can rest.
Chapters: 01:02 Arrival and settling 02:20 Relief and embodiment 05:04 Blessing what remains 09:53 Body and mind together 15:05 Clinging and loss 21:32 Less entanglement 23:34 Receiving the moment
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17744

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting the Winds of Feeling Blow
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on feeling as something to trust rather than manage or explain. He begins by inviting a careful “Dharma posture,” then gradually turns toward the body’s surges of emotion and impulse, framing practice as learning to let feeling move through without immediately turning it into story. Near the end, he links that surrender to confidence in Buddha, Dhamma, and Sangha, so the practice lands in faith rather than effortful control.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 00:54 Dharma Posture 03:19 Moved by Goodness 05:36 Wisdom and Love 12:54 Feeling and Freedom 14:28 Letting Feeling Pass 17:13 Not Making Meaning 23:26 Surrender and Refuge
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17632

item.title: Guided Meditation: Beginning with Goodness
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with the suggestion that practice can start from a felt sense of goodness already present in experience. Matthew Brensilver then repeatedly returns to the breath as a place of receiving, releasing, and settling into care without strain. The closing section brings the meditation into a larger view, suggesting that the sacred does not need to be manufactured and can be recognized in ordinary life.
Chapters: 01:24 Beginning with goodness 04:38 Settling in the body 10:54 Breath as nourishment 18:09 Relaxing vigilance 19:55 Devoting ourselves here 27:33 The sacred already present
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17637

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting the Breath Come to You
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation that begins by noticing what happens when we "sit together" and then shifts toward resting in what cannot be stopped. He gradually distinguishes the breath as a gentle object of attention while also emphasizing a broader openness in which awareness already receives everything. The practice returns again and again to the breath as something ordinary, nourishing, and not separate from the rest of life.
Chapters: 00:12 Arriving Together 01:28 What Meditation Does 04:41 Resting In Openness 06:51 Open Awareness 11:38 Starting With Wise View 12:42 Breath As The Circle 15:51 Receiving The Breath 20:10 Nothing Outside Is Suspicious 21:24 Gentle Inclining
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17638

item.title: Guided Meditation: Self and Space
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation built around non-striving, resting in awareness, and easing the habit of making something out of the moment. He gradually moves from the simplicity of being alive, through repeated rests and reflections on self-making, toward a practice of leaning into what does not depend on the self. The talk closes by widening this into a daily practice of sincerity and asking, moment by moment, what opens space now.
Chapters: 00:00 Simple aliveness 02:57 Giving up acquisition 07:49 Rest where awareness stays 15:59 Putting down life 20:11 Hindrances and disenchantment 24:27 How self is reiterated 29:26 Rest in dependence 34:15 Love and surrender 42:07 Practice in daily life
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17174

item.title: Guided Meditation: Affect and Motivation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with posture, breath, and a felt sense of the body as a constellation of sensations rather than a fixed outline. He then turns toward affect and motivation, encouraging close attention to the body’s signals of wanting, aversion, and urgency without preferring spaciousness over solidification. In the later teaching, he links clinging to these affective circuits, points to how narratives try to explain experience, and returns to the value of staying with practice, equanimity, and the pace of awareness.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling into posture 02:30 Breath and relaxation 03:58 Body as sensation 08:51 Softening the heart 12:29 No preference for spaciousness 20:57 Affect and motivation 26:52 Knowing and letting go 35:31 Clinging and narrative 46:23 Practice rhythms and closing
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17161

item.title: Guided Meditation: Attention & Awareness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation that moves from settling into practice to a close look at attention, clinging, and the ease that comes from making peace with the present moment. He distinguishes the “spotlight” of attention from awareness itself, then gradually shifts toward resting in the knowing that cannot be turned off. The talk is quiet and reflective, with extended meditation space and a final emphasis on relinquishing control.
Chapters: 00:25 Arriving to meditate 02:22 Natural beginning 08:22 Relaxing and letting go 10:03 Attention settles 14:43 Less control 15:38 Resting in awareness 20:30 Spotlight and clinging 26:31 Unpartitioned knowing
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17013

Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17013

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sound, Outer and Inner
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on listening, beginning with settling and sensing goodness, then opening attention to the outer soundscape. The practice gradually turns toward sound as passing phenomenon, including the mind's own verbal thought, so that thinking can be heard more as another sound than as self. The talk stays close to direct experience and repeatedly returns to letting sounds move through without grasping.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and Settling 02:15 Beginning with Goodness 08:08 Outer Soundscape 14:27 Sound Passing Through 20:08 Hearing Inner Speech 25:45 Thoughts as Sound
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17010

item.title: Guided Meditation: Faith in Awareness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on trust in awareness rather than trying to engineer experience. The practice moves from settling posture and sensing what is present, to recognizing that even ordinary stillness is both familiar and fresh, to relaxing the search for signs of progress. He closes by emphasizing that the seeds of awakening are already here and that the practitioner’s work can become simpler: listen carefully, attune, and rest.
Chapters: 00:12 Entering the sit 01:06 Familiar and fresh 03:17 Meeting this moment 06:23 Dharma posture 12:29 No need for signs 14:43 Faith and trust 21:32 Already enough 28:24 Listen and rest
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/17001

item.title: Guided Meditation: Unseduced by the Mirage of the Future
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a gentle guided meditation centered on loosening the habit of performing in practice and meeting experience without trying to prove anything. The meditation moves from settling the body and attention, to relaxing self-conscious effort, to seeing how the future can seduce the mind into anxiety and projection. He returns again and again to simple phrases of enoughness, encouraging a willingness to rest in what is already present.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and settling 01:19 Natural beginning 04:49 No need to prove 07:06 Attending lightly 12:09 Time and projection 18:49 Unseduced by future 19:58 Enoughness and surrender 25:57 Opening to phenomena
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16996

item.title: Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on letting attention settle into soothing phenomena and the silence behind experience. The practice moves from posture and settling, to finding ease in the body and environment, to resting with whatever arises without needing to fix anything. He closes by reframing practice as a simple Dharma gesture: let time pass, let the teachings unfold, and trust the wholesomeness of intention.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 00:48 Letting Practice Lead 02:18 Opening to Stillness 06:12 Finding Soothing Objects 08:19 Resting In Ease 12:41 Tranquility and Healing 14:43 Goodness of Intention 19:55 Time As Ally
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16992

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that moves from settling the body into a spacious way of meeting sound, sensation, and emotion. The talk gently reframes bracing and aversion as habits of protection, then invites a more trusting openness in which experience can arise without being organized around a defended center. In the second half, the practice softens toward a sustained sense of awareness as something that can hold and mellow the forces of suffering.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 02:23 Welcoming Experience 04:14 Breath and Ease 08:48 Letting Winds Blow 12:15 Grasping and Aversion 16:38 No Need To Pretend 22:49 Softening The Heart 27:49 Sustained Open Awareness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16985

item.title: Guided Meditation: Effort and Effortlessness
item.description:
This guided meditation moves from settling the body into a simple, lightly held practice of attention. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes the difference between effortful attention and the more effortless quality of awareness, using the breath or another anchor as a relaxed point of return rather than something to force. Later, he invites a softer relationship to experience, including a memorable instruction to let the face fall off as a way of loosening identification and resting in openness.
Chapters: 02:54 Landing and settling 06:26 Choosing an anchor 13:12 Effort and awareness 18:56 Relaxing the grip 20:17 Less mesmerized 25:01 Letting the face soften 27:38 Opening further
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16824

item.itunes:summary:

item.title: Guided Meditation: Getting Somewhere by Going Nowhere
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores the paradox of meditating with aspiration while letting go of striving. He starts by contrasting goal-directed effort with a quieter, atelic way of practicing, then moves into a guided opening that settles attention into the body and softens grasping. From there the talk widens into a contemplation of awareness, liberation, and the way practice privileges presence over the relentless need to get somewhere.
Chapters: 00:27 Goals and Dukkha 01:56 Telic and Atelic 03:01 One Moment at a Time 05:29 Aspiration Without Urgency 08:38 Settling Into the Body 15:33 Liberation in Phenomena 17:58 Relaxing the Struggle 23:29 Be Blissful
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16821

item.title: Guided Meditation: Meditation as an Atelic Activity
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver begins with a philosopher’s critique of goal-directed living and connects it to the Buddha’s teaching on dukkha: the strain of always completing one thing in order to chase the next. He then turns that frame toward meditation, emphasizing that practice is not only a tool for improvement but also a chance to step out of striving and rest in present-moment experience. The guided portion keeps returning to the same point: the breath, the body, and even thinking itself are reminders to loosen achievement and let this moment be enough.
Chapters: 00:55 Goals and Their Limits 02:22 Dukkha and Fatigue 03:02 Meditation Beyond Goals 04:21 Arriving in the Body 05:08 Settling and Surrendering 11:39 When Striving Returns 13:27 Breath as Reminder 17:27 Thoughts Beside the Point 24:29 Rest in Imperfection
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16817

item.title: Guided Meditation: No Fidelity to the Past Moment
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation built around the phrase “no fidelity to the past moment,” beginning with a short reading from Catherine Schultz about the mixed and changing nature of emotion. He then turns that passage into a Dharma point: practice does not try to preserve a mood or force a state to continue, and attention can stay open to both continuity and discontinuity. The sitting unfolds into instructions about landing in the body, relaxing the urge to narrate experience, and meeting whatever comes next with freshness and intimacy.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening Welcome 00:44 Reading on Mixed Feeling 03:52 No Fidelity to the Past 05:34 Settling Into Practice 10:48 Listening With Spaciousness 13:58 Freshness and Discontinuity 25:22 Moment-by-Moment Offering
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16811

item.title: Guided Meditation: Landing in the Body Breathing
item.description:
This guided meditation emphasizes landing attention in the body and breathing, then using love and awareness to meet whatever arises without turning experience into an enemy. Matthew Brensilver gently widens the practice from the body to the emotional patterns behind worry and the urge to fix and secure life, then returns again and again to the breath. The talk closes by framing mindfulness as a deep surrender that privileges awareness over projects.
Chapters: 00:13 Opening and settling 02:10 Love as reference 05:13 Breath through the body 07:28 Gentle discipline 13:07 Meeting the urge to fix 16:06 Returning to breathing 21:23 Mindfulness as surrender
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16813

item.title: Morning sit with instruction (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided morning sit centered on three facets of practice: attitude, attention, and awareness. The instruction moves from goodwill and bodily softening into several ways of placing attention—breath, whole body, visual field, and soundscape—while repeatedly returning to the easeful knowing that does not depend on effort. Later he turns to questions from retreatants, especially around pain, eating, and walking meditation, and emphasizes gentleness, imperfection, and the limits of control.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and orientation 00:44 Three facets of practice 04:20 Softening the body 08:14 Breath and stability 19:14 Whole-body and sound 29:50 Resting in awareness 36:17 Questions on practice 43:56 Eating and wanting 53:34 Walking and closing
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/72188/

item.title: Guided Meditation
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with settling the body and attention, using Thich Nhat Hanh’s image of nectar sinking to the bottom of a glass to suggest a natural rather than forced calm. Matthew then moves between gentle instruction and long stretches of silence, first inviting a more precise focus and then a wider opening to whatever needs patience, love, and rest. In the closing section, he names the effort involved in directing attention and points toward an effortless dimension of awareness that does not need to be propped up.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and settling 02:49 Nectar image 03:13 Precise or open 04:49 Quiet guidance 08:22 Forgiving phenomena 11:05 Deepening rest 18:42 Effort and ease
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16543

item.title: Tuesday Sit with Instructions: Affect and Meaning-making
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet instruction-based sit centered on affect: how bodily feeling and the mind’s meaning-making shape one another. He repeatedly returns to softening the face, sensing urgency in the body, and meeting unpleasant feeling without turning it into a story about threat or failure. The talk gradually widens from simple relaxation instructions to a steadier, more ethical view in which equanimity, patience, and non-violence become easier to recognize.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling and Softening 02:21 Body as Attention 07:22 Worry and Threat 11:27 Affect and Awareness 15:39 Urgency and Equanimity 20:19 Feeling Without Story 28:15 Soft Heart, Open Body 36:18 Meaning in Its Place
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16497

item.title: Guided Meditation - Grief and Compassion (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on grief that treats sorrow as a natural part of loving in an impermanent world. The practice moves from settling the body into phrases of tenderness, mercy, and reverence, with brief references to Leonard Cohen, anicca, and Stephen Levine to steady the listener in uncertainty. The tone stays intimate and practical, emphasizing that compassion is not an extra but a necessity when loss keeps arriving.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:20 Settling In 01:27 Welcoming the Body 02:37 Love and Grief 05:40 Impermanence and Tenderness 11:10 Traveling in Fog 15:31 Holding Sorrow 19:10 Love Deepened 20:04 Tender Phrases
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/70749/

item.title: Guided Meditation on Body and Affect (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with relaxing the face, shoulders, and belly, then widens into breath and whole-body awareness. From there, the talk turns toward affect: how feeling signals alarm, how vigilance and grasping show up in the body, and how equanimity can let experience remain without needing to fix it. In the second half, he shifts into retreat practice more broadly, especially the challenge of carrying mindfulness into walking, eating, and the ordinary moments where habits and self-consciousness most easily return.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:01 Relaxing the body 05:14 Whole-body breath 10:31 Smoothing intensity 15:44 Affect and alarm 18:56 Permission to settle 22:02 Releasing grasping 26:32 Continuity beyond the cushion 41:33 Mindfulness and eating 53:31 Retreat instructions
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/70714/

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
This lightly guided meditation invites a gradual softening of vigilance, self-protection, and mental reference points. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the felt sense of this moment, encouraging a relaxation into experience rather than a struggle to manage it. Midway through, he quotes a teacher’s instruction—“Don’t fight with yourself at any level”—and uses it to open into radical permission, intimacy, and equanimity.
Chapters: 00:57 Opening to Experience 01:58 Safe Enough to Melt 02:42 Letting Armor Unwind 06:02 Dropping Reference Points 10:00 No Fighting 15:50 Tolerating Not Knowing 20:30 Resting the Heart
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16271
