item.title: Guided Meditation: Non-possessiveness and Joy
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation on non-possessiveness and sympathetic joy, beginning with settling the body and softening the mind’s need to sort every problem at once. The practice moves from releasing hope and fear into letting go, then into mudita: becoming porous to the happiness of others without comparison or threat. The closing invites an expansive wish for joy to increase for all beings, including those not yet born.
Chapters: 00:25 Settling In 00:55 What Is Here 02:38 Putting Down the World 03:38 Hope Into This Moment 08:26 Letting Go and Joy 11:08 Porous to Others 14:56 Wishing Happiness Well 20:43 No Self, Just World 24:26 Extending the Wish
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16265

item.title: Guided Meditation: Compassion
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on compassion that begins with making space for the full range of human joy and sorrow, then settles into a steadier confidence in taking one’s seat amid ordinary imperfection. He gradually turns toward the suffering of someone cared about, first allowing grief to be felt and then shifting into warmth, blessing, and the wish that suffering be eased. The meditation closes by loosening the boundary between self and other and resting in a more open, distinction-less compassion.
Chapters: 00:12 Arriving and settling 01:25 Space for human life 05:24 Relaxing control 08:24 Blessing the moment 14:26 Seeing another's pain 18:39 From grief to warmth 24:01 Offering care 27:24 Open compassion
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16258

item.title: Guided Meditation: Lovingkindness Practice
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided lovingkindness practice that begins by clarifying what metta is and is not: not niceness, and not pretending. He then turns attention to the body as a place of real goodness and generosity, before moving into the core phrases of metta and the felt sense of letting go of self-patrolling, self-hatred, and vigilance. The practice ends by emphasizing that this kindness is not earned but simply offered.
Chapters: 01:08 Metta, Not Niceness 03:38 Kindness Toward the Body 06:40 Noticing Quiet Goodness 16:45 The Bell of Phrases 20:25 May I Be Happy 22:20 May I Be Safe 27:42 Unconditional Offering
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16267

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation that moves from settling the body into awareness to exploring the sense that awareness is always already present. He emphasizes relaxing effort, noticing what cannot be stopped, and resting in the open space in which breath, sensation, and thought arise. Midway through, a repeated invocation of Shantideva supports a spacious turning: instead of strategizing or fretting, one keeps folding life back into awareness.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling In 03:01 Relaxing the Body 05:56 What Can't Be Stopped 07:48 Directing Attention 13:41 Suffering and Identification 16:04 Shantideva Refrain 21:57 Folding Life In 28:43 Closing Bells
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16257

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quietly guided meditation on softening into experience rather than trying to produce a special state. He returns repeatedly to the invitation to make the heart available, and links that availability with gratitude, non-moralistic presence, and a widening sense of innocence. The talk moves from settling the body and breath into a gentler relationship with judgment, aversion, and self-worth, ending with a simple emphasis on love coloring everything.
Chapters: 00:30 Settling In 02:29 Becoming Available 06:47 Each Moment as Gift 11:23 Beyond Judgment 19:13 Basic Innocence 20:25 Releasing the Past 22:00 Awareness Holds It 24:56 Love Flavors Everything
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16102

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation centered on posture, breath, and a gradual softening of effort. He repeatedly returns to the sense that we are not responsible for manufacturing insight or concentration, only for sincerity, willingness, and trust in the Dharma. The talk then narrows into simpler and simpler contact with experience—breath, body, feeling, sound, silence, even darkness behind closed eyes—while emphasizing receptivity over control.
Chapters: 00:20 Settling In 02:21 Soft Exhalation 04:35 Not Responsible for Everything 07:25 Sincerity and Trust 12:43 Simplifying Experience 20:46 Receptivity and Softening 29:13 Surrender in Awareness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16091

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided sitting practice built around stopping, easing in, and noticing how attention can rest without bracing or grasping. He frames meditation as a willingness to tolerate the loose ends of life, then narrows to a simple circle of practice and the question of what falls inside or outside it. As the talk develops, he opens that frame back out: every sensation, even difficulty, can become a door to wisdom, love, and a steadier present.
Chapters: 00:13 Welcome and settling 00:58 Stopping with courage 04:09 Loose ends of life 06:12 Wind through awareness 08:49 Drawing the circle 11:00 Balanced resting 15:34 Everything as a door 23:25 Sentience and love
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16079

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation centered on breathing softly with the body, especially the exhale, as a way to settle intensity without trying to solve life’s larger problems. The practice moves from posture and full breaths into a patient attention to belly, face, jaw, and a general smoothing of bodily density. In the latter part of the talk, he reflects briefly on how emotional activation can drive thought, then returns to extended silent practice with very occasional reminders.
Chapters: 00:25 Settling In 00:55 Full Breaths 02:42 Soften the Body 03:44 Breath Rhythm 04:50 Smooth the Energy 07:56 Just for Now 09:52 Long Silent Practice 14:55 Emotion and Thought 17:24 Lovingly Working With Density
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16074

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation centered on letting attention rest differently: not as a spotlight from behind the eyes, but as the body being pervaded by awareness. He moves through images of earth, vibration, and spaciousness, then gently points to how present-moment experience can loosen control and soften the heart. In the closing reflections, he emphasizes that practice does not remove life’s complexity, but can resolve suffering in this moment.
Chapters: 00:00 Welcome and format 00:56 Settling the posture 02:44 Awareness in the body 05:00 Earth and space 08:43 Control and time 12:55 Letting the sit be 16:31 Softening through imperfection 23:19 Confidence in letting go
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16076

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
This talk begins as a lightly guided meditation, using ambient sound, breath, and body awareness to settle the room. Matthew Brensilver then turns toward how the mind keeps wanting to optimize experience, and he gently explores the relief of not sorting every moment into pleasant and unpleasant. The long middle and ending section becomes a quiet, repetitive accompaniment that supports staying with simple presence, though parts of the transcript appear unclear.
Chapters: 00:41 Opening the sit 02:00 Ambient sound and settling 02:49 All phenomena as gateway 05:30 Breath and whole body 08:54 Not optimizing the moment 14:01 Undefended presence 20:10 Extended absorption 27:28 Quiet closing stretch
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16051

item.title: Guided compassion practice: grieving as a factor of growth
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided compassion practice that moves from settling the body into a reflection on grief as part of spiritual growth. He frames grief not as a failure of practice, but as a natural companion to love, ethical maturity, and a deeper willingness to care. The meditation then expands from holding one suffering person to radiating compassion more broadly, with repeated phrases and a closing blessing for all beings.
Location: Insight Meditation Society - Retreat Center
Chapters: 00:30 Settling In 02:01 Growth and Grief 07:09 Waking Up to Loss 09:25 Dharma and Generativity 11:36 Compassion as Action 14:44 Taking in Suffering 20:27 Grief and Karuna 26:05 Heart Practice 34:17 Boundless Well-Wishing
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/69766/

item.title: Guided meditation: feeling as a prediction of risk
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with a long settling into the body, using the breath, posture, and soundscape as a stable anchor while gently releasing bracing. It then turns toward Matthew Brensilver’s central theme: feeling as a prediction of risk, including urgency, aversion, and bodily pain, and how equanimity can keep those signals from turning into extra struggle. The closing section opens into brief questions about walking meditation, then returns to the practical tone of staying close, sensitive, and steady in retreat.
Location: Insight Meditation Society - Retreat Center
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the body 04:09 Finding an anchor 10:00 Object and background 17:05 Feeling as threat 24:13 False alarm 27:42 Opening to feeling 35:10 Pain and aversion 40:49 Walking and equanimity 55:14 Closing retreat instructions
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/69754/

item.title: Guided Metta practice
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided metta practice that begins with a quiet reflection on why lovingkindness matters, especially when the alternatives are hatred, apathy, or self-judgment. He then moves into a steady seated practice with phrases for self, other, and all beings, while emphasizing that metta can work as cultivation, concentration, and purification. The talk stays grounded in the ordinary conditions of human life, showing how tenderness can include whatever is present rather than wait for a perfect feeling state.
Location: Insight Meditation Society - Retreat Center
Chapters: 00:00 Why Metta Matters 04:53 Three Functions 09:13 Settling the Body 13:41 Self-Compassion Phrases 19:48 Extending to Others 24:53 Warmth and Care 30:01 Pain as Practice 34:01 All Beings at Ease 39:01 Closing
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/69738/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting Go Cultivates Stability
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a gently guided sit centered on landing in the body, gathering attention with the breath, and loosening the habit of trying to improve the moment. The talk moves from settling and gratitude into a clear explanation of why letting go can stabilize attention rather than weaken it. It then opens into an extended period of loving-kindness phrases, ending with the suggestion to trust awareness instead of endlessly optimizing experience.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling In 05:24 Breath as Home 11:21 Letting Go of Better 17:21 Truce with Imperfection 23:40 Loving-Kindness Phrases 29:10 Trusting Awareness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/14661

item.title: Guided Meditation: Don't Let Greed And Aversion Lurk Behind the Technique
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with a caution: reaching for technique can sometimes be a subtle way of avoiding greed, aversion, or clinging already present. He then leads a breath practice that becomes increasingly precise and steady, while naming the pull of worry, strategizing, and affect beneath thinking. The talk returns again and again to making peace with whatever is already here so the breath can feel like shelter rather than escape.
Chapters: 00:45 Technique and avoidance 02:14 A subtle warning 04:00 Settling the body 06:29 Precise attention 11:28 When attention wanders 18:42 Shelter and release
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/14568

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not Reiterating or Reimaging Self
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a quiet shift away from managing experience and toward simply resting in awareness. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the theme of not optimizing the moment, not building out a self-story, and not rehearsing a body-image or room-image that reinforces separation. The practice moves from settling the body and setting intention into a softer surrender of the autobiographical mind, with attention to urgent energy, spaciousness, and a final emphasis on trusting awareness without figuring anything out.
Chapters: 00:37 Settling In 01:54 Awareness First 05:21 Resting Without Agenda 09:52 Softening Self-Image 12:46 Autobiography in Awareness 18:28 Meeting Urgency 20:09 Space Moving Through 27:12 Nothing To Figure Out
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/14570

item.title: Guided Meditation: Clinging Complicates Everything
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation on how clinging tightens experience and makes the mind feel complicated. He begins with breath and body cues for smoothing out energy, then turns toward greed, aversion, and the ways practice can become simpler when there is less grasping. The later part of the talk points toward resting more fully in the moment, with a brief Ajahn Chah reference shaping the theme of simplicity and letting go.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling In 02:54 Balancing Energy 05:50 Breath Through the Body 09:27 The Cost of Clinging 12:15 Equanimity and Ease 14:13 Resting in Simplicity 22:12 Sacred Enough
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/14560

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sound and the Openness of Awareness
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with settling the body through softening and relaxation, then gradually turns toward sound as a way to steady attention. Matthew Brensilver invites listeners to notice not only the arising and ending of sound, but also the spacious, receptive quality of awareness that can hold it all. The practice moves from directed attention to a quieter resting in awareness itself.
Chapters: 01:02 Settling In 02:58 Softening the Body 08:02 Alert and Soft 10:15 Entering Soundscape 13:58 Sound Fading 18:03 Resting in Awareness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/14558

item.title: Guided Meditation: Grounding and Opening
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with a brief framing of the week’s theme—bringing Dharma into interpersonal life—before moving into a long practice of settling, grounding, and opening. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the body, gravity, the exhalation, and a softening of effort, then widens into an awareness that can hold whatever is known. The talk gradually turns toward relinquishing fixing and becoming, treating imperfection itself as part of the path toward peace.
Chapters: 00:12 Opening Context 02:57 Settling Into Body 05:47 Awareness and Softening 10:02 Holding Imperfection 12:23 Returning to Ground 18:43 Letting Dharma Unfold
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/14556

item.title: Guided Meditation: Resting in Goodness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation oriented toward resting in goodness: recalling the wholesome qualities already present in one’s life, feeling gratitude for the Dharma lineage, and settling attention into the soundscape. The practice then turns toward a more open field of sound, silence, and the ease of not clinging. Near the end, he emphasizes that the heart needs rest and that relaxing into our human imperfection is not indulgent, but necessary.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and settling 01:21 Remembering goodness 02:46 Lineage and gratitude 04:42 Resting in blessing 12:36 Sound as one field 13:47 Deepening into ease 24:47 Letting the heart rest
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/14008

item.title: Guided Meditation: Being Changed by Experience Rather than Changing it
item.description:
This guided meditation centers on allowing experience to alter us rather than trying to manage or shape it. Matthew Brensilver begins with posture and relaxation, then gradually turns attention toward the felt sense of experience, the urge to push and pull, and the willingness to be impacted. The practice settles into a quiet instruction to stand down, feel through urges, and let the heart be softened by what is already here.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and arrival 01:15 Finding the posture 03:53 Releasing tension 06:54 Softening toward experience 13:27 Feeling at the center 15:27 Willing to be changed 18:38 Standing down 20:29 Closing turn
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/13999

item.title: Guided Meditation: Softening to the Ungovernability of Experience
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on softening to the ungovernability of experience: meeting discomfort without needing to organize, fix, or sort it all out. The practice moves between settling the body, feeling the weight of gravity, and opening to the reality of dukkha with a steadier, more tender mind. He returns again and again to the possibility that equanimity is not cold distance, but a quiet route into love.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening Reflection 02:24 Settling Into Stillness 05:43 Body and Gravity 08:05 Not Sorting It Out 11:05 Freedom In Experience 16:13 Bowing To Dukkha 17:06 Silent Practice 30:15 Closing Bells
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/13994

item.title: Guided Meditation: Between Suppression and Entanglement
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation that moves from settling the body into sensing companionship and refuge. He emphasizes softening self-conscious tension, resting in the goodwill of Sangha, Buddha, and Dharma, and letting awareness bless experience without forcing it. The practice then opens into a spacious release of struggle, ending with a middle way he names as neither suppression nor entanglement.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening Welcome 00:39 Sustainable Posture 02:02 Presence and Softening 04:26 Relaxing the Body 08:08 Undefended Refuge 12:09 Blessing Experience 19:35 Releasing Strife 25:41 Middle Way Closing
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/13989

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not Fighting with Ourselves
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on equanimity as a kind of nonviolence toward experience. He frames the practice as not fighting with the nervous system, softening around collisions of pain and contraction, and making peace with the human condition rather than trying to force experience to be אחרת. The meditation gradually opens into a reflection that equanimity does not make life colder; it can make the heart more responsive to both suffering and joy.
Chapters: 00:12 Welcome and frame 01:26 Equanimity theme 02:05 Non-interference 03:03 Sustainable posture 05:47 Practice for all 09:22 Softening around collision 11:44 Renouncing grasping 20:18 Letting go in practice 28:04 Equanimity deepens life
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/13982

item.title: Guided Meditation - changing conditions
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by settling the body and noticing how silence feels, with an emphasis on allowing experience rather than forcing a particular inner posture. It then opens into investigation of hope and fear, dukkha, and the way the mind resists impermanence, using images of water, river, and flow to point toward anicca. The closing movement is gentle and practical: loosening the story of self, resting in change, and recognizing how that can support a more open love.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:01 Settling In 01:38 Silence and Temperature 02:54 Hope and Fear 06:25 Body and Sensitivity 10:16 Opening to Dukkha 13:35 The Weight of Change 21:20 Anicca in Experience 29:25 River Without Banks 38:32 Letting Go to Love
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/68413/

item.title: Reflections and Guided Heart Practice
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens with a brief communal reflection on the brahma-viharas and then turns toward why loving-kindness matters in practice: not as a pleasant mood, but as a softening of the heart. He connects this to a Buddha story about the “abodes of Brahma,” then reads Raymond Carver’s “Late Fragment” to widen the question of what it means to feel beloved on the earth. The second half becomes a guided loving-kindness practice centered on receiving kindness, first from imagined benefactors and then, if it feels right, extending it outward.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:01 Opening the Heart Practice 00:33 Community and Receiving 05:06 Kindness Over Attention 09:01 Brahma-viharas Explained 11:35 Freedom and Tenderness 13:51 Raymond Carver's Question 16:29 Metta as Free Heart 20:03 Settling and Imagining 24:31 Receiving Kindness
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/68409/

item.title: Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation framed by brief reflections on why practice can be simple, restful, and honest about suffering. The talk moves from settling into the body and breath into a wider instruction on renunciation, boredom, and continuity of awareness in movement and daily life. He repeatedly returns to non-striving: just practice, just arrive, and let mindfulness build its own momentum.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Practice as Simplicity 06:58 Settling In 08:25 Body and Breath 17:21 Relaxation and Release 24:36 Returning Again 35:50 Offering the Heart 40:30 Rest and Intensity 42:10 Walking and Continuity
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/68404/

item.title: Friday Morning Instructions: The non-compulsion of awareness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet instruction on how awareness can hold experience without being driven by it. He moves from settling into posture and breath, to the way habit and memory color the present, to a more somatic recognition of clinging as pressure, urgency, and drivenness in the body. The talk then widens into a gentle reframe of liberation, ending with a reminder that the Dharma is flexible and that practice is about noticing what is skillful now.
Chapters: 00:30 Settling In 03:40 Breath and Body 07:53 The Past in the Present 15:31 Awareness Without Compulsion 24:21 Clinging in the Body 34:15 Freeing Experience 40:24 Putting Down Judgment 48:34 Skillful Means
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/12561

item.title: Wednesday Morning Instructions: Clarifying Body
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided body meditation that begins with settling into surrounding silence and then turns toward gravity, exhalation, and the changing texture of sensation. The talk gradually reframes the body as a shifting field rather than a solid object, encouraging a truce with imperfection, a softer relationship to wanting to fix experience, and a steadier equanimity. In the closing minutes, he narrows the instruction to “protecting nothing” and carrying a thread of Dharma into the day through non-distractedness, tranquility, or mindfulness.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling Into Silence 03:59 Gravity and Exhalation 09:07 Whole-Body Awareness 11:44 Unifying Attention 15:14 Clarifying Body Experience 26:05 Truce With Imperfection 36:14 Dharma Mind 40:25 Protecting Nothing 48:14 Carrying Practice Forward
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/12545
item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided body meditation that begins with settling into surrounding silence and then turns toward gravity, exhalation, and the changing texture of sensation. The talk gradually reframes the body as a shifting field rather than a solid object, encouraging a truce with imperfection, a softer relationship to wanting to fix experience, and a steadier equanimity. In the closing minutes, he narrows the instruction to “protecting nothing” and carrying a thread of Dharma into the day through non-distractedness, tranquility, or mindfulness.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling Into Silence 03:59 Gravity and Exhalation 09:07 Whole-Body Awareness 11:44 Unifying Attention 15:14 Clarifying Body Experience 26:05 Truce With Imperfection 36:14 Dharma Mind 40:25 Protecting Nothing 48:14 Carrying Practice Forward
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/12545

item.title: Monday Morning instructions
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens this retreat-day talk by reflecting on what meditation is for: rest, refuge, insight, steadiness, renunciation, and a more honest relationship to suffering and freedom. He then shifts into practical instruction for sitting and walking practice, emphasizing softness, continuity, patience, and a gentle willingness to meet boredom, clinging, and the changing conditions of the mind without forcing outcomes. The talk closes with encouragement to treat each moment of mindfulness as the next support for practice, rather than judging the whole day by how it feels.
Chapters: 00:00 What meditation is 06:33 Technique and trust 07:28 Settling the body 12:14 Truce with effort 22:18 Awareness of sensation 37:29 Breath and basic aliveness 44:01 Continuity in walking 51:02 Another moment of mindfulness
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/12526

item.title: Guided Meditation: Solid, Liquid, Gas
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on meeting experience without agenda, beginning with a gentle invitation to welcome what is here and to put down the burden of self-improvement. The practice then moves into an embodied reflection on impermanence, using the language of solid, liquid, and gas to show how suffering, craving, breath, body, and self-view continually change. In the final movement, the talk opens toward compassion, suggesting a tender way of meeting what feels heavy rather than trying to force it open.
Chapters: 00:00 Arriving Without Agenda 02:23 Putting Down the Story 05:30 Allowing What Remains 11:13 Solid, Liquid, Gas 16:25 Anicca in the Body 27:12 Nothing to Hold 36:57 Compassion for the Heavy 38:51 Closing Bells
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/12263

item.title: Guided Meditation: Radical Permission to Arrive
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on "radical permission": beginning by settling attention in the room and in the body, then widening into the idea that nothing in experience needs to be excluded. The instruction moves from breath and posture into a spacious acceptance of thought, feeling, and sensation, before returning to practical guidance for walking meditation and continuity of mindfulness through the day. The tone is gentle and steady, with repeated emphasis on allowing what is here and trusting that practice can be both simple and complete.
Chapters: 00:01 Arrive in Space 02:26 Nothing Excluded 05:21 Permission to Be Here 11:50 Radical Self-Acceptance 21:22 Breath Trains the Mind 40:21 Gently with the First Day 47:24 Walking as Continuity
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/12257

item.title: Guided Reflection & Meditation: Grief & Compassion
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet reflection on grief as part of loving well, moving from metta into compassion, impermanence, and the moral work of not hardening around pain. He speaks personally and also broadly about social suffering, then grounds the talk in a guided phrase practice and a reading of Hala Alyan’s poem "Spoiler." The ending returns to letting go as a form of freedom, not denial.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Opening Into Practice 01:36 Metta Meets Suffering 03:02 Groundlessness and Grief 05:15 Why We Grieve 08:44 Softening the Heart 13:42 Generative Grief Practice 22:38 Reading Hala Alyan 27:46 Letting Go Freely
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/63042/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Feelings and their elaborations
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation focused on feeling tone: pleasant, unpleasant, and neutral, and how quickly those tones can turn into story, aversion, and identity. The guidance stays close to direct bodily experience, repeatedly returning attention to breath, posture, sensation, and the simple fact of what is present. He closes with a brief Rumi quote and a short Damasio passage that reinforce the teaching that feeling accompanies mind throughout ordinary experience.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Arriving 01:58 Attention as care 08:33 Body first 17:42 Feeling tone 24:32 From sensation to story 36:35 Not building on feeling 39:30 Rumi and transition 49:38 Damasio and closing
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/63036/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Settling into the Body
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that moves gently from settling the body to trusting the breath as a stable cue. He emphasizes that resting is not negligence, but a way of loosening compulsive worry without losing care or ethics. The practice widens into a shared retreat intention, a softening into gravity and nature, and a patient trust in the Dharma’s logic.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Opening Instructions 01:37 Taking Cues From Body 04:01 It Is Okay To Rest 05:10 Shared Intention 08:03 Gravity And Release 11:20 Natural Breathing 17:30 Porous To Breath 27:58 Innocent Mind Movements 29:58 Trusting The Dharma
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/63029/

item.title: Day 6 Morning Guided Instructions: Pervasiveness of Feeling (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
In this guided morning instruction, Matthew Brensilver points the listener toward a quieter allegiance to awareness than to the ordinary pull of life. He builds the practice around feeling: how it shapes thought, how it generates suffering and motivation, and how meeting it directly can soften rather than harden the heart. The talk moves from settling the body and grounding in gravity, into a close investigation of pleasant and unpleasant feeling, and then into a steadier confidence in non-abiding and radical permission.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Choosing Awareness 02:52 Taking The Seat 08:10 Why Investigate 12:04 The Power Of Feeling 15:04 Ajahn Chah's Instruction 21:23 Finding Feelings In The Body 26:58 Thought And Cascade 34:07 Blooming And Collapsing 38:57 Radical Permission
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/59145/

item.title: Day 2 Morning Guided Instructions (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This guided morning sit invites rest as something available even when experience feels imperfect. Matthew Brensilver moves from relaxing the body and settling into sensations, to tracking the breath with steadiness, and then into a broader trust in the Dharma’s timing and unfolding. The closing section turns practical, with retreat logistics and a brief emphasis on affinity groups and shared practice support.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 01:01 Rest Is Available 04:11 Scanning for Bracing 09:17 Settling With Gravity 13:58 Breath in the Body 20:38 Sustaining Attention 25:13 Letting Go of Sorting 32:45 Falling Into Awareness 38:20 Trust and Practice Logistics
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/59015/

item.title: Morning Instructions
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a grounded morning instruction centered on paying attention to life as it is, with care rather than ambition. He moves from meeting hindrances and “Mara” directly, to sensing the body as a changing field, to the Buddha’s teaching on conditionality as the basis for a more natural love. The talk closes by noticing subtle efforting in meditation and returning to curiosity without agenda.
Chapters: 00:00 This Life, Attended To 02:27 Meeting Hindrance 04:39 Directness Over Struggle 07:53 Seeing Conditionality 10:48 Body As Flow 14:27 Image And Sensation 18:17 No Hidden Agenda
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/10634

item.title: Guided Meditation: Body and Space (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
A mostly guided meditation that begins with settling into the breath and a soft body scan, then gradually widens into mindfulness of sound, space, and the changing texture of experience. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes ease, honesty, and not pretending, suggesting that mindfulness makes room for what is present rather than pushing it away. The close turns toward impermanence: sensations, self, and even identity as process rather than fixed things.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:01 Settling with breath 06:50 Relaxing effort 10:04 Simple presence 20:26 Letting go again 25:43 Body and space 32:41 Everything is process 38:40 Closing
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/57123/

item.title: Guided Meditation: How the world impacts the emotional body
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with a brief reflection on how the body carries emotional response, then moves into a long period of practice centered on breath, hands, and the felt sense of emotion. He emphasizes that sound, memory, pain, irritation, grief, and restlessness all register in the emotional body, and that meeting those sensations with steady awareness can soften the heart and make mindfulness a refuge. The talk closes by widening the teaching into daily life, including urgency, self-judgment, and the idea that practice unfolds in many chapters rather than one fixed method.
Location: Vallecitos Mountain Retreat Center
Chapters: 00:00 Opening reflection 02:46 Clinging and the body 06:48 Making peace with feeling 11:38 Mindfulness as refuge 13:48 Breath and hands 18:29 Emotional circuits 24:06 Permission and contact 29:08 Extended stillness 40:18 Grief and daily life
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/56694/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Body Settling, Pleasure of the Breath
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by meeting the fear and uncertainty that can arise at the start of retreat, and gently reframes practice as a movement toward love and safety. Matthew then leads a body-based settling, balancing alertness with relaxation, and gradually narrows attention to the breath. The talk emphasizes subtle pleasure in breathing, the ways thought pulls attention away, and the invitation to let go without forcing anything.
Location: Vallecitos Mountain Retreat Center
Chapters: 00:00 Meeting Fear with Love 03:28 Posture and Grounding 04:42 Warming the Body 09:08 Finding the Breath 11:40 Sustaining Contact 14:21 Pleasure in Breathing 20:58 Thought and Letting Go 22:44 Simple Return 29:11 After the Bell
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/56638/

item.title: Guided Meditation: The Ground of Loving Acceptance
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation on how the sense of self keeps seeking ground through activity, and how that search can soften when met with loving acceptance. The practice moves from noticing strain and self-fixing, to resting in breath and body, to working directly with feeling tone and the emotional body as the place where clinging gathers. Near the end, he turns toward ease, non-striving, and a quieter relationship with whatever is present, closing with a Mary Oliver line about letting the body love what it loves.
Chapters: 00:01 Selfing and strain 02:11 Loving acceptance begins 03:05 Finding the ground 06:28 No pressure to be someone 14:14 Feeling tone and clinging 18:39 Breathing into feeling 25:17 Softening the body 34:55 Ease and closing
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/10078

item.title: Guided Meditation: Settling through Love, Deepening through Sound
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver guides a quiet meditation that begins with reflection on love as the force that holds life together and gradually turns toward felt experience, sound, and awareness. The practice moves from softening the heart to opening the field of listening, including the inner sound and the way thoughts can be heard as sound. In the final section, he returns to refuge, self-story, and a sustainable way of practicing without heroics.
Chapters: 00:00 Arriving Through Love 01:42 Softening the Heart 09:18 Kindness Without Pretense 13:36 Opening to Sound 27:18 Sound as One Field 38:32 Rumi on Wholeness 42:17 Refuge and Self-Story 46:05 Resting Sustainably
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/10073

item.title: Guided Meditation: Settling into the Pleasure of the Breath
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation that begins with settling the body and sensing the breath as a source of quiet pleasure. The practice then widens into a way of using that pleasure to signal safety, soften the heart, and stay with experience without sorting it into good and bad. In the latter part of the talk he turns to walking meditation, describing it as a bridge into daily life and a place to work gently with boredom, continuity, and attention.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the body 02:36 Pleasure in the breath 07:10 Letting awareness flood 11:32 Safety and softening 19:27 Whole-body presence 29:35 Knowing the breath 33:18 Walking as continuity 40:29 Working with boredom 45:00 Practical retreat notes
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/10070

item.title: guided meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on settling the nervous system, stabilizing attention, and meeting the usual friction of practice with patience. He moves from posture and breathing instructions into ways of working with fatigue, agitation, and the tendency to make a meditation into a future project. The talk gradually widens into a gentle inquiry into self, fear, and the quiet that can come when attention is steady enough to rest.
Location: New York Insight Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the posture 01:28 Entering practice gently 05:36 Root and support 08:16 Breath and energy 11:53 Making peace with imperfection 19:04 This life, not rehearsal 23:36 Choosing the field of attention 27:40 Steadying the inner gaze 32:57 Scanning for clinging
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/55368/

item.title: Sitting with Instructions (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver frames retreat practice as a way of fully digesting experience rather than adding something to a life already elsewhere. He moves from the ordinary senses and the mind’s movement, to love as the deeper measure of awakening, to the quiet freedom that comes when practice becomes inseparable from life. In the second half, he turns toward retreat ending: change, integration, tears, and the way a practice can continue to settle after the formal sitting is over.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 05:01 Experience as life 08:57 Digesting the day 14:35 Love as measure 25:33 Awakening into love 34:27 Subtle freedom 38:51 Endings and change 43:12 Dreams and letting go 48:08 Tears and integration
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/53260/

item.title: Day 6: Sitting with Instructions (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet instruction for sitting with honesty rather than performance. He moves from noticing urgency and defining the edge of attention, into a longer reflection on impermanence, where the present moment is not something to freeze but something to trust as it changes. The talk then turns toward mettā practice and closes with a practical reminder about continuity of awareness in ordinary life.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 01:16 Starting the sit 01:51 Not pretending 07:31 Drawing the boundary 15:24 Urge and tension 23:25 No banks on this river 32:44 Offering experience 37:43 Turning to mettā 48:16 Continuity off the cushion
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/53239/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Turbulence and Peace (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver begins with a personal story about crying in a dark breathing group, using it to explore how grief, laughter, and a deep peace can coexist. He then moves into a practical account of mental noting, clinging, and the way practice reveals both the movement into selfing and the possibility of letting go. In the second half, he responds to questions about retreat energy, secular mindfulness, ethics, renunciation, and free will, ending with a quiet preference to keep trying without overclaiming certainty.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Grief and Peace 09:55 Noting Experience 16:08 Craving and Selfing 24:20 Letting Go 34:01 Retreat Energy 40:53 Mindfulness and Renunciation 49:39 Agency and Trying
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/50735/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Clinging and Space (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with settling the body, scanning for how the retreat moment feels, and reassuring the mind that the conditions for freedom are already here. It then turns toward attention, letting go of what is not ours, and meeting craving, aversion, and imperfection with steadiness rather than resistance. In the later sections, Matthew opens out into compassion and nonviolence, drawing on Buddha teachings, Martin Luther King Jr., Shinzen Young, and the Dalai Lama to show how softening the heart can change how we meet experience and one another.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 05:11 Finding the Anchor 12:35 Letting Go 18:29 Craving and Aversion 29:02 More Space Than Expected 39:58 Body and Thought 46:18 Nonviolence and Love 52:37 Silence and Integration
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/50674/

item.title: Talk: Guided Meditation: Insight Into Not-self
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores not-self as a lived practice rather than a philosophy, beginning with how we freeze loved ones, and ourselves, into familiar concepts. He moves through mental noting, the burden of selfing, and how clinging, fear, and self-protection make experience feel dense and brittle. The talk gradually opens into a calmer view in which impermanence loosens identification, and not-self becomes closely tied to love, freedom, and care for others.
Chapters: 00:00 Seeing Beyond Concepts 03:36 Noting and Self Assumptions 09:09 Not-Self as Practice 13:39 Ego, Shame, and Comparison 17:59 Curating the Self 24:57 Thought and the Sense of I 32:29 Narrative Gravity and Fear 41:37 Open Air of Change 46:53 Not-Self and Love
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/8750

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting Go of Becoming
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on how mindfulness can soften the urge to become someone, acquire something, or fix experience. The practice moves from simple questions about what it means to stop meditating or stop being still, into resting with safety, silence, and awareness, and then into a more pointed reflection on clinging to the future and the self. Later, the tone becomes more intimate and paradoxical, with reminders that suffering matters and doesn’t, that we can’t fill the heart’s hole from the outside, and that love remains after letting go.
Chapters: 00:00 Entering Stillness 03:57 Safety and Simplicity 06:47 Awakening as Letting Go 13:06 Space Through the Mind 18:27 The Heart's Hole 24:24 Love After Letting Go 36:15 Closing Praise
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/8747

item.title: Guided Meditation: Emotions and Inner Safety
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on meeting emotion directly as bodily sensation, rather than turning it quickly into story, judgment, or strategy. He keeps returning to the theme of inner safety: staying close to the feeling body, softening around pain, and learning to receive pleasant and unpleasant states without clinging or bracing. Near the end, he broadens the practice into everyday life, using a brief story about a man living on the streets to show how "my problems" can look different when awareness is immediate and spacious.
Chapters: 00:01 Opening reflection 07:11 Emotion in the body 10:39 Mindfulness and equanimity 17:05 Receiving emotion gently 24:32 Body and mind 29:22 Radical permission 37:34 After the bell 42:27 Where are my problems? 46:37 Closing encouragement
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/8743

item.title: Guided Meditation: Settling, Body, Breath
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation that moves from settling the body to resting with the breath, then into walking practice and the continuity of mindfulness through ordinary movement. He repeatedly invites a softer relationship to experience: relax the face and belly, let the body be dignified, and stop needing to sort every moment into right or wrong. The talk leans on practical instructions, with a few larger reflections on how practice can include boredom, uncertainty, and the mind’s habit of trying to protect itself.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the mind 03:22 Modest responsibilities 04:47 Relax the face 11:09 Upright and soft 19:06 Making peace with imperfection 24:54 The breath at close range 40:04 Retreat as cooking 50:31 Walking and continuity
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/8739

item.title: Matthew Brensilver, Sylvia Boorstein: Day 2: Evening Practice Session (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This evening practice session opens with Sylvia Boorstein gently gathering the retreat together at the end of a few days of silence, then moves into a close reading of the Metta Sutta as both poetry and instruction. The talk lingers on how lovingkindness is not just a feeling but a disciplined way of seeing suffering clearly, withholding ill will, and training the mind through repetition. It ends by widening the circle to difficult people and to Martin Luther King Jr.’s language of loving enemies, linking compassion with freedom and steadiness.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Additional teachers: Sylvia Boorstein
Chapters: 01:30 Opening the evening 02:58 Reading the text together 15:44 Metta as instruction 20:00 Pair work and daily use 28:57 Repetition and reflection 58:16 Suffering and consequence 01:13:48 Benefits of metta 01:19:48 Enemies and freedom 01:23:34 Closing sit and walk
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/48356/

item.title: Matthew Brensilver, Sylvia Boorstein: Day 2: Afternoon Practice Session (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This afternoon practice session moves from a simple story about family irritation into a wide-ranging reflection on attention, suffering, and the way practice changes how life is seen. Sylvia Boorstein uses stories from ordinary life, death, and retreat practice to show how clarity can cut through editorializing and reveal what is actually happening. The talk closes by returning to loving-kindness in movement, with walking practice turned toward wishing well for everyone encountered.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Additional teachers: Sylvia Boorstein
Chapters: 00:00 Practice as attention 08:36 Widening the field 22:57 Retreat and first tears 44:51 Inching into Dharma 55:22 Perspective and mortality 01:06:56 Clarity and the heart 01:18:57 Renunciation and dying 01:29:55 The path in practice 01:35:08 Walking with goodwill
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/48355/

item.title: Matthew Brensilver, Sylvia Boorstein: Day 2: Morning Practice Session
item.description:
This morning session begins with movement and posture instruction, then widens into a meditation on how the body can serve as a direct doorway into present-moment awareness. From there, the talk turns toward loving-kindness practice: simple phrases, the Metta Sutta, and the ways metta can include strangers, difficult people, and the whole world without forcing feeling. The closing stretch reflects on mindfulness, ethics, and whether insight is mature only when it also deepens decency, care, and responsiveness.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Additional teachers: Sylvia Boorstein
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and schedule 01:01 Tsunami and timing 13:07 Body as entry point 22:42 Qigong and grounding 33:38 Posture and support 43:46 Letting go into love 01:14:13 Metta and universal goodwill 01:41:48 Mindfulness and ethics 02:58:54 Guided loving-kindness
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/48343/

item.title: Matthew Brensilver, Sylvia Boorstein: Day 1: Afternoon Practice Session (part 1) (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This retreat session offers a patient, practical talk on how the mind gets pulled into stories and how practice can return it to what is actually happening. Sylvia Boorstein uses vivid metaphors—movie houses, realms, walking down Broadway—to point toward steadiness, while Matthew Brensilver joins in to expand on working with pain, emotional reactivity, and the difference between enduring and injuring oneself. The talk gradually moves from basic sitting instructions to a gentler view of suffering, vulnerability, and the softening that mindfulness can make possible.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Additional teachers: Sylvia Boorstein
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the mind 01:37 Walking toward clarity 03:31 Sitting instructions 22:27 Naming the breath 32:48 Body awareness cue 34:31 Working with pain 54:57 Emotional movie loops 01:00:08 Wounds and compassion 01:07:22 Out of the realm
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/48322/

item.title: Matthew Brensilver, Sylvia Boorstein: Day 1: Evening Practice Session (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This evening session moves from a short period of sitting into a broad Dharma teaching on training the mind, the Four Noble Truths, and the Eightfold Path. Sylvia Boorstein then traces the path through story, personal memory, poetry, and examples of everyday generosity, showing how letting go, grief, and compassion are part of the same practice. Matthew Brensilver joins near the end to reflect on non-clinging, and the talk closes with a quiet emphasis on freedom from ownership and neediness.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Additional teachers: Sylvia Boorstein
Chapters: 00:00 Opening instructions 01:53 Shared practice 49:49 Buddhist basics 01:03:11 Practice stays human 01:13:18 Paramis and early inspiration 01:15:37 Compassion and non-clinging 01:18:24 Letting go of ownership 01:29:53 Freedom through giving 01:36:58 Moonlight and closing
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/48332/

item.title: Matthew Brensilver, Sylvia Boorstein: Day 1: Afternoon Practice Session (part 2) (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Sylvia Boorstein offers a practical talk and guided sitting on how to meet ordinary afflictive states without self-criticism. Using humor, poems, and simple breath instructions, she turns annoyance, sleepiness, restlessness, and doubt into occasions for steadier attention. The talk gradually widens from personal discomfort to compassion, civic engagement, and the ordinary courage of continuing to practice.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Additional teachers: Sylvia Boorstein

Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/48328/

item.title: Matthew Brensilver, Sylvia Boorstein: Day 1: Morning Practice Session (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This retreat opening moves from love and mindfulness into a clear description of why practice matters at all: life is intense, thought makes it seem more urgent than it is, and retreat gives us a chance to meet experience more directly. Matthew Brensilver and Sylvia Boorstein alternate between teaching, short guided periods, and conversation, returning again and again to softness, equanimity, and a truce with imperfection. The talk includes references to Daniel Kahneman, After Life, Martin Luther King Jr., the Metta Sutta, and the Foundations of Mindfulness, each used to steady the practical arc of the morning rather than decorate it.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Additional teachers: Sylvia Boorstein
Chapters: 00:00 Opening to practice 10:17 A moment to live forever 14:09 Equanimity and attention 44:51 Softening into the body 01:26:51 Walking with goodwill 01:42:36 Dukkha and hardening 02:31:54 Not to control life 03:01:43 Practice, food, and closing
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/48321/

item.title: Matthew Brensilver, Sylvia Boorstein: Opening Night: Evening Practice Session (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This opening-night retreat talk sets a quiet frame for the weekend: a shared container of silence, care, and practical training. Sylvia Boorstein introduces the teaching team, then the talk moves into simple practices of blessing, warm-hearted intention, and meeting experience fully and as a friend. Matthew Brensilver later widens the frame to refuge, the precepts, and a steadying phrase—“tranquil and alert”—as portable supports for retreat and daily life.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Additional teachers: Sylvia Boorstein
Chapters: 00:31 Welcoming the retreat 03:51 Retreat instructions 08:03 Seeing the people here 16:58 Meet it as a friend 39:15 Skillful response 45:37 Collective refuge 50:38 Precepts and safety 01:01:38 Portable practice phrases 01:06:26 Tranquil and alert
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/48314/

item.title: Guided Meditation: The Clouds are Sky Too
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on relaxing effort and resting with awareness rather than clinging to the meditation object. The practice moves from settling the body and mind into a direct inquiry into present-moment experience, including a repeated reflection on whether anything is really being waited for. As the meditation unfolds, he uses the sky-and-clouds image, then turns it inside out, inviting a softer sense that experience itself can be held with permission and ease.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling with awareness 05:49 Not waiting anymore 10:09 Safety in the present 15:41 Permission to arise 25:34 Radical acceptance 31:54 Sky and clouds 37:55 Rumi's ocean image 41:00 Putting down effort
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/8129

item.title: Day 5: Instructions and Sitting
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a mostly quiet sitting instruction centered on relaxation, receptivity, and letting awareness be present without effort. He begins with Suzuki Roshi’s phrase “soft readiness,” then moves into guidance about releasing doing, dropping concern with the next breath, and trusting the present moment. Later he turns to the seductions of time and future-imagining, ending with a closing invitation to give oneself more fully to the last minutes of sitting.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Soft readiness 00:40 Settling the body 07:22 No need to perform 09:01 Letting the moment be 20:31 Time and safety 22:32 Returning to sitting 34:23 Final release
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/41171/

item.title: Day 4: Metta Sit with Guidance (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This guided sit explores mudita, or sympathetic joy, by moving from a simple story about delighting in a child’s imagination to a careful look at how envy and comparison arise. Matthew Brensilver then leads practice first with an easy person, then with someone who triggers envy, framing that reaction as a clue to self-clinging rather than a personal failure. The talk closes by softening the heart toward oneself and letting the well-being of others feel less threatening.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Opening into practice 02:59 What mudita means 04:35 Meeting envy 07:11 Envy as teacher 08:30 Settling the body 12:59 Joy for a dear person 19:51 Turning toward challenge 24:01 Working with comparison 29:53 Opening the heart
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/41144/

The talk closes by softening the heart toward oneself and letting the well-being of others feel less threatening.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Opening into practice 02:59 What mudita means 04:35 Meeting envy 07:11 Envy as teacher 08:30 Settling the body 12:59 Joy for a dear person 19:51 Turning toward challenge 24:01 Working with comparison 29:53 Opening the heart
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/41144/

item.title: Day 3 Instructions and Sitting (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided retreat sit that begins with simple attention on breath and sound, then widens to emotional feeling and the bodily patterns that carry it. He धीरे moves into the experience of internal dialogue, showing how thoughts are heard, how they interact with feeling, and how attention can rest with either one without trying to push experience away. The talk stays practical and steady, emphasizing allowance, contact, and equanimity rather than control.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Simple Attention 05:47 Not Bracing 09:02 From Breath to Feeling 11:37 Body of Emotion 16:14 Rolling Tides 23:37 Equanimity and Softening 24:37 Listening to Thought 30:24 Hearing the Narrator 33:08 Feeling and Talk Together
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/41139/

item.title: Day 1: Instructions and Guided Meditation (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens this retreat talk by naming the day’s structure, then offers a simple framework for understanding mindfulness through three mechanisms: attentional control, emotion regulation, and self-awareness. He grounds each one in direct practice, moving from the breath to working with distraction, emotion, and the quiet loosening of identification. The talk then turns into a guided meditation, with instruction on relaxing the body, opening to breathing, and using gentle mental noting to steady awareness.
Location: Spirit Rock Meditation Center
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the Retreat Day 02:44 Three Ways Mindfulness Works 05:15 Training Attention 08:59 Meeting Emotion Clearly 11:45 From Identity to Awareness 14:22 Settling the Body 20:58 Opening to Breath 25:42 Gentle Noting 31:01 Sustaining Simplicity
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/41098/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Breath
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by inviting a gentle, nonjudgmental relationship to experience and then settles into the breath as a simple place to collect attention. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly encourages curiosity, noting, and quiet returning, while also normalizing distractions, thoughts, and feeling as part of being human. In the later part of the practice, he widens the field again, softening effort and ending with whole-body awareness and letting go.
Chapters: 00:00 Arriving Kindly 03:39 Simplifying Attention 06:31 Breath as Object 10:38 Noting In and Out 16:18 Returning Gently 22:05 Winds of Experience 27:15 Letting Go Wider
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/7544

item.title: Morning instructions - Breath and Body
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers morning retreat instructions centered on breath, body, and how to meet whatever arises without turning experience into a problem. He emphasizes that discomfort is neither a personal failure nor random, then keeps returning to the simplicity of resting attention on the breath, including the exhale, the body’s downward settling, and the quiet work of inclusion. In the second half, he broadens the instructions into walking meditation, describing posture, pace, and how walking can support the same continuity of mindfulness as sitting.
Chapters: 00:01 Opening the retreat 01:30 Discomfort and conditioning 03:31 Kindness and simplicity 08:29 Working with the exhale 13:32 Breath counting practice 20:05 Including everything 26:05 Rilke and persistence 31:36 Settling into silence 37:13 Noticing without judging
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/7181

item.title: Guided Meditation (Day 6): Poems, Reflections, Silence
item.description:
This guided meditation moves from poems about sorrow and emptiness into a quiet practice of carrying what is heavy without closing the heart. Matthew Brensilver returns several times to refrains about surrender, softness, and the redemption of pain through love and understanding. The later part settles into long stretches of silence and ends with Rumi’s urging toward wholehearted devotion.
Chapters: 02:00 Sorrow and Emptiness 05:02 Open the Heart 08:44 Lord of Song Refrain 18:18 Softening the Heart 25:24 Pain Redeemed 34:29 Rumi's Invitation 35:44 Closing Silence
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/6630

item.title: Guided Meditation (Day 4): Emotion in the Body
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on meeting emotion in the body with steadiness and kindness. He begins by inviting the listener to stop looking for life elsewhere and to honor what is present right now, then turns toward the physical signatures of emotion—especially in the face, throat, chest, and belly. The practice settles into mindful noting, relaxed alertness, and a spacious awareness that can hold pleasant, unpleasant, and neutral experience without needing to obey every inner command.
Chapters: 00:01 Settling Into Life 02:09 Opening the Heart 07:48 Suffering and Presence 14:40 Emotion in the Body 18:35 Finding the Sensation 22:14 Resting With Change 24:44 Relaxing Effort 28:52 Gentle Noting 30:38 Equanimity and Sky
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/6625

item.title: Guded Meditation (Day 2): Breath
item.description:
This guided meditation starts with a gentle invitation to settle into the breath and notice what the mind brings to stillness. Matthew then moves through simple instructions for receiving the breath, using a light mental note, and softening the habit of trying to improve experience. The talk opens into a quiet reflection on imperfection, kindness, and the freedom that comes from letting the breath stay in the foreground while the rest of experience can remain in the background.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 04:56 Breath as Receiving 11:15 The Cost of Striving 13:30 Meeting Imperfection 25:56 Foreground and Background 32:01 Freedom from Urgency 41:56 After the Practice 46:13 Returning in Daily Life
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/6623

item.title: Morning Instructions - Awareness and Objects
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a morning Dharma instruction centered on relaxing the habit of trying to get somewhere in practice. He repeatedly returns to the simple move of letting experience be known without trying to extract progress from it, then opens into how courage, care, and intimacy with life emerge when identification softens. The talk ends with a short exchange on the phrase “being known,” the use of language in practice, and a quiet closing encouragement for the retreat day.
Chapters: 00:00 The Striving Mind 01:58 Relaxing Into Practice 05:45 No Strings Attached 14:00 Courage and Heart 20:40 Simple, Relaxed, Wise 31:18 Questions on Study 37:21 Being Known 39:03 Language and Right View 44:06 Closing the Retreat Day
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/6348

item.title: Guided Meditation - Awareness and Relaxation
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with a brief framing of practice as awareness of mind and body, then turns toward Buddhism’s concern with suffering and release from it. Matthew Brensilver describes suffering as a kind of claustrophobia and invites a simple but radical gesture: let thoughts, sensations, sounds, and emotions be known. The meditation settles into repeated return to the breath and body, emphasizing that what is known does not have to be fought, and that awareness can feel like refuge.
Chapters: 00:01 Practice Framing 01:03 Suffering and Release 02:40 More Than the Corner 04:00 Knowing What Arises 05:15 Settling Into Refuge 10:43 Returning Again 25:04 Trailing Audio
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/6346

item.title: Guided Meditation: Mindfulness of Mind
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on mindfulness of mind, moving from familiar self-descriptions toward direct noticing of images, words, and thought. He repeatedly invites the listener to see that it is safe not to know, and to rest in the awareness that receives experience rather than in the stream of narration. The practice leans gently into silence, showing how stepping off the ground of thought can soften urgency and change the felt shape of experience.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 02:25 Fresh Seeing 07:50 Breath Body Emotion 10:03 Trusting Not Knowing 13:16 The Mind's Security 19:29 Images And Words 25:02 Silence As Ground 31:22 Stepping Off 39:13 Closing Silence
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/5728

item.title: Walking Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a short, practical introduction to walking meditation, treating it as an extension of sitting practice rather than a break from it. He emphasizes staying with the sensations of the feet, noticing boredom and the mind’s search for stimulation, and being aware of how visual impressions affect attention. The talk ends with a light reminder, attributed to Suzuki Roshi, that practice can be very important without becoming heavy or self-serious.
Chapters: 00:09 Welcome and sign-up 00:55 What walking practice is 01:56 Going nowhere 02:43 Reaching down 03:05 Seeing and association 03:58 Feet and toe 04:30 Practice without strain 04:48 Bell and closing
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/4495

item.title: Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation and teaching on working with anger by breaking experience into three strands: body sensation, mental images, and internal talk. He explains why that distinction matters, then leads a practice that moves through settling the body, noticing emotional sensation, listening to thought, and observing images. The talk stays practical and grounded, with a quiet emphasis on curiosity, steadiness, and not being swept into reactivity.
Chapters: 00:12 Welcome and purpose 01:21 Breaking experience apart 03:31 How anger tangles 06:45 More manageability 08:23 Settling into practice 14:27 Feeling in the body 23:25 Listening to thought 29:17 Images and integration
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/4040
